Elon checked the turn after the fourth heart appeared, which might indicate he lacks a heart or is playing a small one cautiously. There is no value in betting here because a hand without a heart is unlikely to call, and any hand with a heart will certainly call or raise and beat me. Checking is the most prudent action to reach showdown cheaply or evaluate based on his sizing if he bets.

River 3s is a blank that doesn't complete new draws significantly and pairs low cards. My 8d2d remains pure air: no pair, flush, or straight, with ~0% showdown equity against Flash's check-check range. His flop check-call to my c-bet on 6h9hTh (draw-heavy) followed by check-check turn on Ah (flush completes) polarizes his range: nut flushes (e.g., KhQh, JhQh, any Ahx hearts), strong made hands like sets (66/99/TT), T9s/T6s two-pair, or very weak showdown trash/missed draws (overpairs QQ/JJ/KK unlikely but possible, bricked non-heart draws). Checking river shows weakness or trapping monsters, but monsters would often bet turn/river for value. Position allows me to check and win vs his trash (missed draws/overpairs that check back), inducing if he were to bet but he checked. Bluffing has low fold equity: he'll call bets with flushes (~30-50% of range), top pairs, or hero-call wider fearing my value range (e.g., perceived flushes from my flop bet). Pot 1450, deep stacks; betting risks losing 700-1450+ to calls/raises with no equity/backdoor, while checking loses nothing extra and wins pot ~40-50% via showdown. History shows Flash check-calls light but folds rivers sometimes (e.g., Hand 72), but flush board caps bluffs. Pot control preserves stack for 69 more hands; exploitative GTO favors checking air here post-check-check.

I’m on the button with A♠5♠ in a 3‑handed pot (stack 2850 ≈ 28.5BB). This is a very playable, positionally advantaged hand — suited ace with blocker to other aces and decent postflop playability. Facing only the blinds I want to take the initiative, isolate, and use fold equity rather than just calling and letting the big stacks dictate action. A standard 2.5–3x BB open is appropriate here: big enough to punish loose blind defense and keep the pot manageable with my ~28BB stack. I’ll raise to 300 (3x BB). If I get called I’ll use position and c-betting on favorable textures; if I face a large 3‑bet I’ll re-evaluate based on sizing (fold to heavily polarized 4‑bets, consider shove or call vs medium 3‑bets depending on odds). Immediate action: raise to 300.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace-Ten suited, which is a very strong hand in three-handed play. Minni, who is on the button with a relatively short stack of 2,550, has raised to 300. I want to raise here to value-build against her range and potentially isolate her by forcing the Big Blind (Elon) to fold. A raise to 900 puts significant pressure on Minni's stack and allows me to either take the pot down now or play for her remaining chips on the flop if she calls.

Preflop in BB with KJo offsuit facing a button open to 300 and SB 3bet to 900. Pot 1300, to call 800 (good pot odds but OOP postflop). SB 3bet range vs button open is strong (QQ+, AKo, some bluffs/suits), KJo has poor equity (~30-35% vs typical 3bet range) and plays poorly multiway OOP if Minni continues. Minni short-stacked (25bb) at 600 to call after my action, may jam or fold. Raising risks cooler or domination, calling induces multiway pots awkwardly. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots in long game (150 hands).

Flash 3‑bet me to 900 after my 300 open. I’m on the button with A♠5♠ and have 2,550 (≈25.5BB). Folding here is too tight; a shove or thin 4‑bet is tempting but Flash is very deep and aggressive (238BB) so a shove will be called/lightly exploited too often. Calling keeps the pot smaller, preserves my positional advantage and puts me in a good spot to realize equity with a suited ace. Pot is 1,300 now; calling 600 more (900 total committed) gives ~31.6% pot‑equity requirement which A5s has reasonable chances against a wide 3‑bet. I’ll call to 900 and play the flop in position rather than commit now.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace-Ten of hearts in a 3-bet pot. The flop is 5h 6d 4d, which is a very coordinated board that generally favors the caller's range (which includes more middle pairs and suited connectors) over my 3-betting range. Minni has a very short stack remaining (1,950) relative to the pot (1,900), resulting in a Stack-to-Pot Ratio (SPR) of approximately 1. On this board, I have two overcards and a backdoor nut flush draw, but no direct pair or straight draw. If I bet, I am essentially committing to her stack. Given how well this board connects with a button-calling range (77, 88, 67s, 56s, 34s, 78s), checking is the most prudent play. Checking allows me to see a free turn if she checks back, or evaluate her action if she decides to shove her remaining chips. Given the low SPR, I am not looking to bluff into a board that hits her range so hard.

I have top pair (5s) with the nut ace‑kicker on 5♥6♦4♦. Flash was the preflop 3‑better and then checked the flop — his range contains a lot of missed broadway hands and medium pairs, plus some draws. With only ~19.5 BB effective (stack 1,950) and the pot already 1,900, any small c‑bet both gives draws good odds and allows an aggressive, deep opponent to shove or float. Shoving now maximizes fold equity, denies equity to diamond/straight draws and many unpaired hands in his 3‑bet range, and is the simplest +EV line with my strong but vulnerable one‑pair hand. If called I’m committed; if he folds I win the pot immediately. Pot math: calling my shove would cost 1,950 to win 3,850 (≈50.6% equity needed), so many bluffs/overcards will fold. Action: shove all‑in for 1,950.

I am in a 3-bet pot with Ace-Ten of hearts. The flop of 5h-6d-4d is extremely coordinated and connects heavily with Minni's likely range for calling a 3-bet (middle pairs, suited connectors like 78s or 67s). After I checked, Minni shoved her remaining stack, which is roughly the size of the pot (SPR ~1). While I have two overcards and a backdoor nut flush draw, my current hand is only Ace-high. Against her range, which likely consists of made pairs, sets, or open-ended straight draws, I am a significant underdog. Even against her semi-bluffs (like diamond draws), I am often flipping at best. Given the board texture and her stack-commitment, I do not have the required 33.6% equity to justify a call here.

I have Ac5c on the button with a stack of 23,850. The pot is currently 150. Minni is in the big blind with a very short stack of 3,750, and Elon is in the small blind with 12,250. Ace-five suited is a strong hand in three-handed play, especially from the button. I want to put pressure on the blinds and potentially win the pot pre-flop or set up a post-flop situation where I have the advantage. A standard raise to 300 (3x the big blind) is appropriate here to narrow the field or take down the blinds immediately.

9s4h is a complete trash hand with no high card value, connectivity, or suitedness. Facing a button raise from Flash, who likely has a wide range, but my hand has negligible equity and playability out of position in the SB. Pot odds to call 250 into 450 are poor given the expected post-flop disadvantage against Flash's range and Minni potentially defending. Minni's short stack (3750) doesn't change the fact that folding is the correct, disciplined play to preserve stack for better spots. No value in raising as a bluff due to position and hand strength.

I call to 300 total (200 more). A♥9♥ is too strong to fold vs a single-button 3bb open — suited ace with a decent kicker and good postflop playability. The pot odds on a 200 call into ~450 are ~3.25:1, and A9s has solid equity versus a standard button raising range. I prefer to keep the pot manageable, realize equity, and use position postflop (bet/raise on favorable A/heart boards, pot-control or fold on dry / highly coordinated boards). A tiny 3-bet to 500 is possible but it’s a small sizing that can create awkward spots against a deep-stacked, aggressive opponent, so calling is the more +EV line here.

Check. On K♠ 2♠ 7♦ I only have A♥9♥ — Ace-high with a backdoor heart but no pair and no spade protection. Villain’s button opening range will c-bet this board a high percentage and contains many Kx/Ax/broadways that rarely fold to an OOP lead. With pot = 650 and my stack 3550 (SPR ≈ 5.5) I want pot control and to realize equity rather than bluff OOP. Plan: check and see turn. If Flash checks back I get a free card; if he bets a small-to-medium c‑bet (~≤1/2 pot) I’ll call and re-evaluate on the turn; if he barrels big (> ~1/2–3/4 pot) I’ll fold unless I improve (A/9/heart) or gain a clear reason to continue. No check-raise — not a good spot to manufacture fold equity with only Ace-high.

I am the pre-flop aggressor with Ace-high and a backdoor club draw. The flop of K-7-2 is relatively dry and generally favors my perceived range as the button raiser. Minni checked to me, and since she is short-stacked, a continuation bet can often win the pot immediately against hands that missed. I will bet 300 into the 650 pot, which is a standard sizing to maintain pressure while staying relatively safe if she has a King or better.

Call 300. Flash opened wide from the button and now bets 300 into a 650 pot (pot is 950 after the bet). Calling 300 gives me ~24% pot equity required (300/(950+300)=300/1250≈24%). A♥9♥ blocks a lot of Ax combos, has decent showdown value versus a BTN c‑betting range (Kx, Ax, broadways and some bluffs), and has backdoor heart potential. I checked the flop to control the pot and realize equity; raising here with just Ace‑high would have very little fold equity and can get me in awkward spots versus a deeper, aggressive opponent, so a flat call is the +EV line. Plan: call and re‑evaluate on the turn — if I pair an Ace/9 or pick up a heart I can proceed for value; if the turn bricks and Flash barrels big (>~3/4 pot) I will concede and fold, while calling smaller turn bets to realize equity and use position if he ever checks back.

Check. I have A♥9♥ on a 2♠ K♠ 7♦ 2♣ board — Ace-high only, no spade protection and no heart improvement. I called the flop to control the pot and realize equity versus a wide BTN raising range. The turn pairs the board (second deuce) which helps villain's value combos (Kx, any 2x that now looks stronger) and reduces my fold equity. Pot = 1,250 and my stack = 3,250 (SPR ≈ 2.6), so there isn't much room to bluff-raise out-of-position without a real hand. Best line is to check and keep the pot manageable: if Flash checks back I preserve showdown chances; if he bets small-to-medium (roughly ≤ ~1/2 pot) I will call to realize equity and continue versus bluffs/value-separations; if he barrels big (≈ >3/4 pot) I will fold since Ace-high has poor showdown value and is unlikely to be good. Betting here as a bluff is low EV — too many hands will call and I risk committing too much with nothing. So check and re-evaluate to his action.

The turn has paired the board with the Deuce of Clubs. I currently hold Ace-high. Minni called my continuation bet on the flop, which indicates she likely has a piece of the board (like a King or a Seven) or a spade draw. Given that she checked again on the turn and is playing a relatively short stack, I want to avoid being check-raised off my equity. By checking behind, I control the pot size and get a free look at the river, where I might improve to the best hand with an Ace or potentially win a showdown against missed draws.

I rivered two pair (A♠9♠? — actually A♥9♥) on 2♠ K♠ 7♦ 2♣ A♦: that’s Aces and Deuces. Opponent (button) opened pre, c‑bet the flop and checked the turn — a line consistent with a wide, somewhat weak-to-medium range (Kx, Ax, single pair hands, missed draws, occasional pocket pair). My hand is value versus the many Kx and worse one‑pair hands that will check‑call the turn and will call a small/medium river bet. I am, however, crushed by any hand containing a deuce (2x → trips/full house), AK (makes A+K two‑pair that beats A+2), or sets/full houses (KK, 77, AA, 22 pocket combos) — those combos exist but are a minority of the button’s check‑back range. Betting extracts value from Kx and other worse hands, denies a free showdown to some missed draws, and is the best way to grow the pot when ahead while keeping loss on bluffs/strong hands manageable. I will make a value bet sized to get called by worse (roughly half the pot) but small enough to avoid committing my stack to a raise; if Flash raises strongly I will fold (most raises here are likely value/full houses/trips). Action: bet 625 into 1250 pot (≈1/2 pot).

On the river, the Ace of Diamonds provides me with top pair. Minni leads out with a 625 bet into a 1,250 pot. Throughout the hand, Minni has played passively, calling my pre-flop raise and my flop continuation bet before checking the turn. The Ace on the river is a card that hits my perceived range as the pre-flop aggressor, but her lead indicates she either has a King, a weak Ace that just improved, or is potentially bluffing with a missed spade draw. Since I have top pair, folding is not an option. However, raising is risky because she is likely only calling with hands that have me out-kicked (like AJ or AQ) or trips. Calling allows me to see the showdown and win against her bluffs or worse value bets like a King while minimizing losses against better Aces.

3-handed on the button with A2o. Excellent position with initiative postflop. A2o blocks AA/AK/AT and plays decently when called due to ace high and backdoor potential. Minni is relatively short at ~38BB, incentivizing folds. Flash deep but button steals frequently succeed in short-handed play. Raising to 3x BB (300 total) is standard to steal blinds or build pot in position. Folding too tight; limping weak. History shows aggression from blinds but button advantage prevails.

Facing Elon’s standard 3BB open to 300 from the button while I’m in the small blind with T♥7♥. Pot is 450 and it costs me 250 to call. Although T7s has some playability, I’m out of position versus the opener (and the big blind who is deep and often defends or 3-bets). Calling invites a multiway pot with a marginal, often dominated hand; the break-even equity if the BB folds is ~36%, which T7s won’t reliably achieve against the button’s opening range. A tiny 3-bet to 500 would be too small to fold out either opponent and risks a larger 4-bet/squeeze from the big blind. With ~38BB effective I’d rather preserve chips for better spots. Fold and wait for a stronger, better-positioned opportunity.

I am in the Big Blind holding As 2s, a strong suited ace. Elon has raised from the Button to 300. Given the pot odds (calling 200 to win a total pot of 450) and the strength of my hand in a heads-up situation against a button range, this is a clear call. 3-betting is an option, but calling allows me to keep the pot manageable while playing a hand with nut potential out of position.

On this flop of Ac-8c-Jd, I have flopped top pair with a weak kicker (As-2s). Elon, the pre-flop raiser, is likely to continuation bet a wide range here. My hand is strong enough to check-call at least one street, but leading out (donking) is generally not ideal against a range that contains many stronger Aces and better Jacks. Checking is the standard play to see how Elon reacts. If he bets, I will most likely call to see a turn, as my hand is too strong to fold to a single bet on this texture.

Preflop raise from button with A2o got one caller from BB (Flash), who now checks the flop. Board Ac8cJd hits my ace blocker strongly, giving ace-high showdown value and blocking top pairs like AA/AK/AJ. Flash's BB calling range is wide but check suggests weakness (no top pair or strong draw). As preflop aggressor in position, standard continuation bet for thin value against weaker aces/draws, protection vs. clubs/spades/straights, and fold equity. Deep stacks favor keeping pot manageable while building. Half-pot sizing (300 into 650) balances value/protection without overcommitting marginal ace-high. Checking risks giving free cards to draws and loses initiative.

On the flop of Ac-8c-Jd, I have top pair (Aces) with a deuce kicker. Elon, who was the pre-flop raiser, has placed a continuation bet of 300 into a pot of 650. While my kicker is weak, folding top pair on this board against a standard button open and flop c-bet would be too tight. Elon could be betting with a wide variety of hands, including lower pairs (8s, Js), straight draws (QT, T9), or flush draws. Raising here might force out his bluffs while getting called only by better hands (Aces with better kickers, two pairs, or sets). Check-calling allows me to keep the pot controlled and extract value from his bluffs on future streets. I will call the 300.

The turn brings an eight, pairing the board. My hand remains top pair with a weak kicker. At this stage, leading out into the pre-flop raiser doesn't accomplish much, as better hands like stronger Aces, Jacks, or an Eight will call or raise, while weaker hands will fold. Checking allows me to control the pot size and gives Elon the opportunity to continue betting with bluffs or draws. I will check and evaluate his response.

Turn pairs the eights, bringing possible straight draws (e.g., 10-9) and completing some flush draws if suited, but my Ad blocks nut club flush while giving top pair blocker. Ace-high remains strong with good showdown value against Flash's BB calling range (worse aces like A10/A9, Jx, mid pairs, missed draws). He checked flop call c-bet and checks turn again, indicating weakness or trapping, but position allows continued aggression for thin value, fold equity vs draws/marginal hands, and protection. Deep stacks (mine 116x BB, his deeper) favor controlled pot-building; checking risks free river cards to draws or bluffs. Half-to-2/3 pot bet (800 into 1250) balances value from worse Ax/Jx while denying equity. History shows Flash calls but folds to pressure (e.g., Hands 10,12,50); opponent tendencies support firing again as preflop aggressor.

The 8d on the turn pairs the board (Ac-Jd-8c-8d). My hand is top pair with the weakest possible kicker (As-2s). Elon is betting 800 into a pot of 1250, which is a significant sizing. While my kicker is weak, folding top pair on this board against a button range is too conservative, as Elon could be semi-bluffing with a variety of draws (flush draws, straight draws like KQ or QT) or value-betting a Jack. I am losing to any stronger Ace or any 8, but I have enough equity and 'bluff-catching' potential to warrant a call to see the river. Raising would be counter-productive as it only gets called by hands that have me beat.

On the river, the board is Ac-Jd-8c-8d-7c. My hand is top pair with the weakest possible kicker (Aces with a Deuce). The board has paired (Eights) and the club flush draw has completed. As the out-of-position player, betting into the pre-flop aggressor who has fired two barrels is risky, as I'm unlikely to be called by worse hands and will almost certainly be called or raised by better hands (stronger Aces, any 8, or a flush). Checking is the most prudent move, allowing me to potentially see a cheap showdown or evaluate Elon's sizing if he decides to bet a third street. My hand has some showdown value but functions primarily as a bluff-catcher at this point.

River 7c completes the nut club flush draw, adding danger, but it's a low card that doesn't improve many straight draws and pairs the board minimally. My Ad2c gives top pair aces with a weak deuce kicker, strong showdown value against Flash's BB calling range (AJ/AQ/AK unlikely given calls, more like mid pairs, Jx, 8x, worse Ax like A10/A9/A8, or missed draws planning to bluff). I've bet flop and turn as preflop aggressor in position, building pot to 2850; Flash's triple check indicates capped range without nuts. Thin value bet extracts from worse top pair/Jx/8x while having fold equity vs pure air/missed draws. Checking risks chopping or losing to bluffs but preserves stack; betting exploits history (Flash calls stations but folds pressure, e.g., Hands 10,12,39,50,72,74,80). Deep effective stacks (~108BB) support controlled sizing. Pot-sized bet too big; ~55% pot (1600) balances value/protection/equity denial, polarizes range to flushes/sets while value-owning weaker hands.

On the river, the board is Ac-Jd-8c-8d-7c. I hold top pair with the weakest possible kicker (As-2s). Elon, the pre-flop aggressor from the button, has triple-barreled, betting 1600 into a pot of 2850 on the river. This betting sequence is highly polarized. The board has paired (Eights) and the club flush draw has completed. While my hand is a 'bluff-catcher', it loses to any other Ace (AK-A3), any Eight (trips), any Jack (two pair), and any flush. Given that Elon has followed through on every street with increasing bet sizes, it is highly likely he is holding a value hand that has my weak Ace beat. While I only need to be right about 26% of the time to justify a call, A2 is at the very bottom of my calling range here, and I expect to see a stronger hand from Elon the majority of the time.

3-handed on the button with 9♦5♠ — marginal hand but highly playable in position. With deep stacks (~38BB) and only the blinds to beat, I want to apply pressure and leverage positional advantage rather than limp. A standard 3x BB open gives good fold equity and narrows opponents’ ranges while still leaving room to maneuver postflop. Plan: open to 300. If I get a single caller I’ll play postflop aggressively with c-bets when I have equity or fold to heavy resistance; if I face a meaningful 3-bet from SB/BB I’ll typically fold 95o (no reasonable 3-bet equity).

I am in the Small Blind with 7c 4d, which is a very weak hand. Minni has raised to 300 from the Button. While Minni's stack is quite short (3525), calling with this hand out of position is not profitable, and 3-betting as a bluff against a short stack who is likely to commit is also unwise. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

T7o is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability out of position. Facing a button raise from Minni, who often raises wide but has shown strength in spots, the pot odds (3.25:1) aren't sufficient to justify calling with such low equity against her range. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

I am on the button with 5s 4d. This is a very weak starting hand that does not play well post-flop and has very little equity. While the button is a good position to steal blinds, 5-4 offsuit is near the bottom of my range. Given Minni's short stack in the Big Blind, they may be looking for a spot to shove, making a steal attempt with trash hands more risky. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

Preflop in small blind, facing big blind only after button folds. My hand Js2d is offsuit, disconnected, and very weak with poor playability post-flop. Equity against a random big blind range is low (~35-40%), and while pot odds to call 50 into 150 are decent (3:1), completing with trash risks difficult spots OOP with Minni short-stacked and capable of shoving. Raising to steal is possible given Minni's history of folding, but J2o performs poorly if called. Best to fold and wait for better spots to preserve stack and avoid marginal situations.

Preflop on the button with K♦9♠, king-high offsuit hand in a limped pot (pot 150, to call 100, likely SB limp and BB check). Excellent position with two opponents ahead who have shown aggression but are vulnerable to steals. K9o plays well postflop in position, blocks strong kings, and has straight potential. Raising isolates the short-stacked Minni (SB ~40BB) and applies pressure on deep-stacked Flash (BB). Standard button raise sizing: 3-4x the BB plus limpers, aiming for 400 total to build pot and take initiative while keeping SPR reasonable. Avoid limping or calling to maintain fold equity and control.

I'm in the small blind (39.8bb effective) with Kc8s facing a button open to 400 and the big blind still to act. K8o is a marginal, often-dominated offsuit hand with poor playability OOP and is unlikely to have the ~39% equity required to call 350 into a 550 pot (350/(550+350) ≈ 38.9%) against a BTN opening range. Calling risks a multiway pot out of position (Flash can still defend) and a small reraise as a bluff (min to 700) is thin — it doesn't give reliable fold equity and would be committing chips with a weak hand. With a healthy stack I want to preserve chips for better spots and avoid difficult postflop decisions OOP, so I fold.

I am in the Big Blind with a pocket pair of deuces. The button (Elon) has made a 4x raise. While pocket deuces are the weakest pair, they have significant value in a head-up pot, especially for set-mining. Given the stack depths (Elon has over 13,000 chips), I have the implied odds to call and try to hit a set on the flop. Folding would be too tight against a button open, and 3-betting is unnecessary with this hand strength and position.

I have pocket deuces on a 10-5-7 rainbow board. As the pre-flop caller out of position, checking to the aggressor is the standard play. My hand has very limited showdown value and will rarely improve, so I am looking to check-fold to most standard bet sizes unless the turn improves my hand or provides a favorable bluffing opportunity.

Preflop raiser with initiative on an uncoordinated, rainbow board (T-5-7) that misses most BB calling ranges but hits some weak pairs/draws. Flash checks, which is standard and weak. My button range is wide and polarized, including overpairs, top pairs, broadways, and bluffs; K9s blocks kings, provides overcard to board, and has backdoor spade potential. C-bet sizing ~60% pot (500 into 850) applies pressure, denies equity to his floats/weak pairs (e.g., 86, Jx), builds pot with equity when called, and maintains fold equity given history of Flash folding to aggression postflop (e.g., Hands 7,17,25). Position allows easy control if checked back. Excellent spot to semi-bluff/value thin.

I am holding the lowest possible pocket pair on a board that contains three cards higher than my pair (10, 7, 5). While Elon's button-opening range is wide, his continuation bet of 500 into an 850 pot is a strong enough signal that I should not continue with a hand that has very little chance of improving and almost no showdown value. I have no draws, and even if I am currently ahead of overcards like QJ or KQ, I am out of position and will face further pressure on later streets. Folding is the most profitable long-term play here.

Button with T♦J♦ in a three-handed pot is a prime spot to take the initiative. This hand plays very well postflop in position and has good equity vs SB/BB calling ranges; I want fold equity and to avoid giving cheap multiway flops. A standard 3xBB open to 300 builds the pot and applies pressure without overcommitting (I have ~40BB effective). If I face a large 3-bet I will re-evaluate (often fold vs very large 3-bets, call smaller 3-bets to realize equity), and if heads-up to a caller I'll c-bet favorable textures and use position to extract value.

In this 3-handed situation, Minni (the button) has raised to 300. I am in the Small Blind with Jack-Six of spades. While J6s is a suited hand that has some playability in 3-handed games, it is generally too weak to call a raise out of position, especially against an aggressive opponent. Minni has a relatively short stack (3,675), which reduces my implied odds if I were to hit a big hand. Additionally, calling puts me at risk of being squeezed by Elon in the Big Blind. Folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my stack for more favorable opportunities.

Ac3s is a suited ace from the BB facing a button raise to 3x. Minni is aggressive and raises frequently from late position with a wide range. Position is poor OOP, but pot odds are favorable (200 to call into 450, ~2.25:1), and there are good implied odds with deep stacks (139bb). The ace blocker and suitedness give playability for top pair potential or flush draws. Folding strong suited aces OOP vs button is too tight; calling to realize equity and control pot size preflop. Will play postflop cautiously.

Flop of 9c 3h 5c is a dream for Ac3s: middle pair (threes) with Ace kicker plus the nut club flush draw (Ac + 9c 5c). Roughly 15 outs (8 clubs + 3 aces + 6 tens for gutshot? Wait, no gutshot, but flush + pair gives ~35% equity vs typical button calling range, even higher vs bluffier ranges. OOP vs aggressive button raiser (Minni frequently raises wide from late position per history). Pot 650, deep stacks (137bb effective vs Minni's ~37bb? Wait, Minni 3675 ~37bb, my 137bb, Flash out). Checking induces a bet from Minni's wide range (misses often, floaty), setting up check-raise for value/protection/build pot. Leading risks check-behind or small bet; check maximizes when she bets 1/2-3/4 pot. Folding impossible, betting decent but check better OOP to realize equity multi-way potential or trap. Plan: check, raise most bets to ~1k-1.5k total.

I opened from the button and got one caller; the flop 9c-3h-5c is very dry and favors the preflop aggressor. T♦J♦ has two overcards and a small backdoor diamond/straight, so a standard continuation bet both takes the initiative and has fold equity against a wide calling/cold-call range. I’ll size about half-pot to apply pressure while keeping the pot controllable. Plan: fold to a large raise (too little equity), call a single raise only in specific spots, and if called re-evaluate on the turn—continue when I improve or get a favorable card/line.

Flop of 9c 3h 5c hits me perfectly: middle pair (3s with A kicker) plus the nut club flush draw (Ac blocks sets too). ~35-45% equity vs Minni's aggressive button continuation range (overpairs, top pair, draws, floats, air). Her small 325 bet (1/2 pot) into 650 suggests weakness or thin value, probing OOP check. OOP postflop, check-raise takes control, builds pot for when we hit nut flush/straights/top pair, protects hand, denies her equity on two club flush draws possible in range (e.g., JcTc, 7c6c, etc.). Her stack 3350 (~33bb), so sizing to ~1200 (3.7x her bet, ~1.2 pot) pressures 28% of her stack while leaving her pot-committed if she jams lighter hands. Calling keeps pot small, allows free cards or her check-back; folding absurd. Deeper stacks allow implied odds, but raising extracts value now from worse made hands/draws. Per history, Minni aggressive but folds to pressure (e.g., Hand 25, 43). Plan: if she shoves, call; turn play depends on board.

Elon check‑raised a small c‑bet to 1,200 on a very dry 9c‑3h‑5c. Pot is 2,175 and I must call 875 (pot odds ≈ 29%). T♦J♦ has two overcards and backdoor diamond equity — against a check‑raise range made up of 9x/sets/middle pairs and some semi‑bluffs (double‑club combos) I estimate ~30–40% equity. That justifies a call: it’s the best way to realize equity in position, keep the pot manageable, and allow me to exploit turn cards. Raising would commit too much with marginal equity; folding is too nitty. Plan: call 875, re‑evaluate on the turn — extract value if I pair or fold to large turn aggression when I miss.

Turn Ah is phenomenal: pairs my Ac for top two pair (Aces and Threes, Ac-Ah-3s-3h), plus nut club flush draw remains (9 outs), backdoor spade straight draw possible. ~70-90% equity vs Minni's flop calling range after my check-raise (Ax like A9/A5/A3 possible but I block A3c/A5c; sets unlikely 9-set/J-set rare; overpairs QQ+ crushed; club draws like QcJc/Tc6c/Jc7c that called raise hoping to realize equity; two-pair 95/53 unlikely). Board scary with clubs, open-enders (67), now heart draws. Pot 3050, Minni 2475 (~0.8 pot), OOP first. Checking risks free card to her draws or check-behind value loss; betting extracts max value from worse Ax/draws/pairs while protecting vs runner-runner. Bet ~80% pot (2400) sizes to her stack, charges max (draws need 20%+ equity they lack vs my range), denies equity. She calls lighter hands profitably for me (history: Minni calls big pots with draws e.g. H32, H77; folds to pressure H25,H43). If jams ahead, hero-call. Shoving overkill (-EV sizing); smaller bet invites cheap river calls. Plan: bet 2400, call jam.

Board is 9c-3h-5c-Ah, my hand Td Jd. Pot 5450, Elon bets 2400; to call I must commit 2400 (leaving me ~75 behind). Pot odds = 2400 / (5450+2400) ≈ 30.6%. My only clean outs are jacks and tens (6 outs) → 6/46 ≈ 13% to win on the river. Elon’s line (check on flop → large check‑raise to 1200 → turn barrel of 2400) is very value‑heavy (9x, sets, some two‑pair/Ax) with a smaller portion of missed club draws/semi‑bluffs. Against that range my equity (~13%) is well below the required ~31%, so a call is a clear -EV. Jamming for the extra 75 chips is unlikely to fold enough of his value range to make it profitable (raise is tiny relative to the pot and he can call cheaply), so it’s not a good bluff. Preserve the stack and fold to avoid getting committed with only 13% equity into a pot where villain’s line indicates strength.

I am on the button with 7h 5c, which is a very weak starting hand. Minni in the Big Blind is short-stacked (approx. 23 BB), which makes her more likely to shove over a button raise. Given the poor equity of my hand and the risk of being shoved on by the short stack, folding is the most disciplined and standard play here.

Preflop in small blind with 67o (offsuit connector). After button folds, facing BB who has a short stack of ~24BB. While pot odds for calling the additional 50 are decent (3:1), playing OOP with this marginal hand leads to difficult postflop spots. 67o has some playability but performs poorly out of position against a defending range. Raising risks getting shoved on by BB's wide defense range given short stack, and calling commits to multi-street OOP play. Best to fold and preserve stack for stronger hands.

On the button in a 3-handed pot pre-flop with K8 suited, a strong speculative hand with position. Pot is 150 with 100 to call (likely blinds posted, SB possibly folded or limped minimally). Excellent stealing spot with fold equity against blinds. Raising standard 3x BB to 300 builds the pot, applies pressure, and leverages position for post-flop play. Hand has good playability with suited connectors potential. No history indicates tight blinds here.

2♣4♥ is a very weak, unplayable hand out of position. Facing a button raise to 300 with a big blind behind, calling 250 more requires ~36% equity (250/(450+250)) to break even — far more than this hand has, especially multiway and OOP. With ~24.8BB effective I should preserve chips for better spots (or 3-bet shove with a much stronger holding). No reason to defend the SB with 2-4 offsuit here, so fold.

I am in the Big Blind with 8-6 offsuit. Elon, the Button, has opened for a 3x raise to 300. While I am getting decent pot odds (200 to call into a pot of 450, roughly 30.7%), 8-6 offsuit is a marginal hand that plays poorly out of position. Elon has shown a high frequency of aggression post-flop in previous hands, and I want to avoid entering a potentially difficult post-flop spot with a weak range. I will fold and wait for a better spot to contest the pot.

I’m on the button with 2c3c and only the blinds left to act. This is a marginal but playable suited connector in late position — I want to use my positional advantage and fold equity rather than limp and invite aggression from two aggressive players. I’ll open to a standard ~3bb (300 total) to steal the blinds or play position postflop. If I face a large 3-bet I’ll fold this hand; if I get called I’ll use position to c-bet favorable flops and realise equity on draws.

I am in the Small Blind with Ten-Six offsuit. Minni, on the button, has raised to 300 and has a relatively short stack of approximately 21 Big Blinds. Ten-Six offsuit is a very weak hand to play out of position, even against a short-stack opening range. Folding preserves my stack and allows me to wait for a stronger hand to exploit Minni's short stack or compete with Elon.

7h 3s is one of the worst possible hands offsuit: no high card value, poor connectivity, no flush potential. Minni raised to 300 from the button, likely a wide range but still strong enough on average. Facing a 200 call into 450 pot (~30% pot odds), but my equity against any reasonable button opening range is under 30%, and postflop playability is disastrous out of position. With deep stacks (mine 15550), no need to speculate. Folding preserves stack for better spots. Consistent with GTO BB defense, which folds 73o cold.

I am on the button with a strong pocket pair (8c 8s). With 3 players left and Minni having a very short stack (around 25 BBs), I want to play aggressively. Elon is in the Small Blind and has a substantial stack. Standard opening size from the button is typically around 2.5x to 3x the big blind. I'll raise to 300 to put pressure on the blinds and potentially isolate.

2h4c is a complete trash hand: offsuit, unconnected low cards, no potential. Facing a button raise from Flash, who has position and a large stack. I'm out of position in the small blind, and Minni in BB has a short stack (2525) that might shove anyway. Pot odds are poor (calling 250 into 450 pot for ~22% equity needed, but this hand has near-zero equity vs a raising range). Folding preserves my healthy 15,500 stack for better spots. Standard discipline pre-flop.

I’m in the big blind with T♦8♥ vs a standard button open to 3bb (300). My stack is ~25bb (2525), pot is 450 and it only costs 200 more to see a flop (300 total). T8o has reasonable equity vs a wide button opening range and the price is cheap — calling 200 into a 450 pot gives ~30% pot odds (200 to win ~650). I don’t want to 3-bet/raise here: a small min-3bet to 500 won’t get many folds from this deep, and shoving or a large 3-bet is too bloated with a marginal hand. I’ll defend the blind, play the flop and only commit further with meaningful improvements (pair, good draw) or a favorable read.

I made the preflop call with T8o and improved to top pair on the Ts-Jc-Ad flop (I hold Td8h). Flash (button) checked to me, which narrows their continuing range toward missed broadways, small pairs and some floats — many strong Ax/Jx hands would have c-bet. I’m out of position and ~23bb effective, so I want to both extract value and deny equity to backdoor draws while keeping the pot controlled. A small lead (~30% pot) is the right line: it wins the pot often vs their checking range, gets value from worse tens/small pairs/draws, and keeps my stack flexible if I face aggression. If I face a large raise I’ll assume I’m beat and fold; if called I’ll re-evaluate on the turn. Betting 200 achieves these goals.

On this flop (Ts Jc Ad), my pocket eights are now essentially a weak underpair. The board is very coordinated and hits a wide range of hands that Minni could be calling with from the Big Blind, specifically any Ace, King, Queen, Jack, or Ten. Minni has led out with a small 'donk' bet of 200 into a pot of 600. Given that Minni is very short-stacked (2125 total), and this board is extremely dangerous for my hand, I have very little equity here and limited room to maneuver. Folding is the most prudent play to preserve my significant stack.

3-handed on the button with SB folded, facing only the BB. Pot is 150, to call 100 for great odds. Qs5d is a decent speculative hand with position, queen-high blocker, and potential for straight/flush draws postflop. Raising small applies fold equity against BB who may fold weak hands, and if called, I have position to realize equity. Standard button steal raise size around 3x BB. Minni short-stacked and folded, Flash deep but history shows frequent folding to aggression. EV positive long-term.

I have JcQd in the small blind facing a button open to 300. Pot is 450 and I need to put in 250 more — calling to a total commitment of 300 gives about 2.8:1 pot odds (≈36% break-even), which is a good price to see a flop with QJo. With ~29BB effective I don't want to 3-bet light out of position and invite a big 3-bet from players who have shown aggression. Plan: call, see the flop, continue with top pair/strong draws or fold to heavy resistance; fold to a large preflop 3-bet. Calling keeps the pot manageable and preserves my stack.

I am in the big blind with K6o. The button (Elon) has raised to 3x, and the small blind (Minni) has called. Minni has a very short stack (around 2675 total). While K6o is not a premium hand, I am getting 3.5-to-1 odds to call (200 to win 700). However, K6o plays poorly in a three-way pot, especially out of position. Elon has been aggressive pre-flop, and Minni's flat-call might represent a wider range or a small pair. Given the pot odds, a call is borderline but acceptable; however, folding preserves my stack since I have a significant lead and want to avoid unnecessary variance against a short-stack jam from Minni on the flop.

Check. I missed this flop with JcQd on Kd-2d-9c — I have no pair and only a runner-runner diamond possibility (very low equity). Elon opened preflop so his range is weighted to Kings and strong broadways; out of position I don’t want to inflate the pot with a marginal hand. Plan: keep the pot small, fold to any sizable continuation bet, and only call very small probes (or if he shows clear weakness). If action checks around I’ll reassess on the turn based on the card and his tendencies.

Preflop raiser on a K-high board with limited coordination (Kd2d9c). My Qs5d has queen blocker, backdoor diamond flush draw, and position. Minni checked OOP after calling preflop, representing a wide range that may include Ax, suited connectors, pairs below K. As aggressor, standard c-bet to protect equity, deny draws, and extract value from worse hands like Qx, JTs, 87s. Half-pot size (350-400) balances value, fold equity, and pot control given her 2675 stack (~25BB). Pot 700 allows this bet without overcommitting.

Elon (button) C‑bets 400 into a 1100 pot on K♦‑2♦‑9♣. I have J♣Q♦ — no pair, only a runner‑runner diamond flush (~4.2% by river) and 6 outs (Qs/Js/Jx) to make at least a pair by the river (~24.2%). Calling 400 to win ~1500 gives pot odds ≈26.7%, so my raw chance to improve is roughly break‑even. Many of those pair outs can still be second best vs Kx/sets, but Elon's small bet size indicates a fairly wide c‑betting range that includes bluffs and hands I can beat. I don’t want to inflate the pot out of position or 3‑bet light, so I’ll take the cheap look at the turn. Plan: call 400, fold to significant turn aggression, and only continue aggressively if I pair the Q/J, pick up a diamond on the turn, or see clear weakness from him.

The turn (8♣) bricks for me — J♣Q♦ has no pair and the runner‑runner diamond possibility is gone. I only have 6 single-card outs to pair on the river (~13%) and most of those will still be behind common c-bet holdings (Kx, 9x, sets). With pot 1500 and my stack 2275, I don’t want to invest more out of position with essentially no equity. Plan: check and fold to any meaningful bet; if Elon checks back we’ll see the river and only continue if I pair J/Q or the board texture gives a clear reason to bluff or call a very small bet.

Preflop raiser continuing aggression on a draw-heavy board (diamond and club flush draws, gutshot straight draws like T7). My Qs5d blocks QJ (open-ender on flop) and has backdoor diamond potential, but primarily bluffing here with position and history of c-betting flop. Minni's OOP check-check after calling flop bet indicates capped range (weak Kx, 9x/8x, missed draws) unlikely to have two-pair+ often. Pot 1500, her stack 2275 (~1.5 pot shove range), betting ~half-pot applies pressure, denies equity to her draws/improves, and exploits fold equity vs short-stacked player who has folded to aggression in history (e.g., Hands 3,6,17). Checking risks giving free card to OESD/club draw and loses value if she has worse pair. Bet sizing balances range including value (KJ, KQ, 99, 88) with bluffs. Long-term EV+ as button aggressor.

Fold. I have J♣Q♦ with no pair on K♦-2♦-9♣-8♣ and Elon has shown aggression preflop, on the flop and again on the turn. The pot is 2,300 and his turn bet is 800 — calling 800 would make the final pot 3,100, so I need ~25.8% equity to break even (800 / 3,100). I only have 6 clean outs (three Qs and three Js) to pair on the river: 6/46 ≈ 13.0%, well below the required equity. Many of those pairs will still be second-best against Kx, 9x or sets, and raising here would be speculative and commit too much of my 2,275 stack. Given the math and his line, this is a -EV spot to call or raise — fold and preserve my stack for better opportunities.

On the button with 6♦3♣ — a very weak, poorly connected, offsuit hand that plays terribly multiway. Pot is 150 and it costs 100 to see the flop. I’m ~22.8 BB effective (2275), not short enough to justify a blind-steal shove and a small raise would be an inefficient use of chips against two live, aggressive blind defenders (Flash SB and Elon BB) who will defend or 3-bet wide. Calling to play postflop multiway with a hand that’s often dominated is a negative expectation. Preserve my stack for a better spot; fold preflop.

I am in the Small Blind with 9s5d. Minni (Button) has already folded. It is just me and Elon (Big Blind) left. The pot is 150, and I need to put in 50 more to call or I can raise. Given 95o is a very weak hand, and Elon is in the Big Blind, I will simply fold to preserve chips, especially as I am currently leading in stack size and there is no need to play marginal hands out of position.

I am on the button with 8d 7d, which is a strong suited connector. With Minni being very short-stacked in the big blind (about 22 BBs), a standard raise to 300 is appropriate to apply pressure and potentially win the blinds, or play a pot in position. I am prepared to evaluate a shove from Minni or a 3-bet from Elon.

Ks2c is a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability and low equity against any reasonable button opening range from Flash, who has been aggressive in prior hands. Facing a raise to 300 from the button, calling 250 more from the small blind offers insufficient pot odds (pot 450, needing ~35% equity) given the hand's dominated nature and reverse implied odds if Minni continues. No fold equity for a squeeze with this hand, especially with Minni short-stacked behind (~22BB) and likely to defend wide. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

Flash opened to 300 from the button. I’m in the big blind with Ah6c and have already posted 100; calling 200 to make it 300 total. Stack = 2,175 (~21.8bb), pot = 450. Calling is the best play here because:
- Pot odds: calling 200 to win ~650 gives me ~23% equity requirement — A6o vs a typical BTN opening range should have usable equity (and the ace blocks many strong Ax combos).
- Position and hand quality: I’m out of position post‑flop, and A6o is marginal for a 3‑bet. A 3‑bet shove or large 3‑bet would be polar and should contain stronger Axs or fold equity tools; this hand is better played by defending.
- Stack depth: ~22bb means postflop play will be shallow; calling keeps the pot controllable and preserves stack for better spots.
Plan: call to see a flop, proceed cautiously OOP — fold to heavy turn/river aggression if I miss, or proceed for value if I pair my ace or hit a favorable board.

Check. I’m OOP with A♠6♣ (actually A♥6♣) on K♣ 9♦ J♥ — I only have ace-high with no realistic draws (a club backdoor is impossible to make a flush). Flash opened preflop from the button, so his C-betting range is heavy in Kx/Jx/broadways and many of those beat my ace. With ~19–22bb effective and a 650 pot, pot control is the priority: checking preserves chips and keeps bluffs/turn decisions manageable. If Flash fires a small probe I can call to see a turn, but I’ll fold to sizeable aggression since I have very little equity unless I pair the ace. If he checks back I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn (only continue for value if I improve).

On this King-high flop with multiple connectors (K-J-9), I have a gutshot straight draw (to a 10) and a backdoor diamond draw. Minni, being extremely short-stacked, has checked. Given the board is quite heavy on broadway cards that hit my pre-flop raising range, I will put out a small continuation bet of 300. This size is effective for folded equity against some of her weaker holdings while keeping the pot manageable if she decides to jam or call with a piece of the board.

I have A♥6♣ on K♣ 9♦ J♥ and I checked the flop. Flash (button) c‑bet 300 into a 950 pot — a small probe that can include both thin value and bluffs. Calling 300 requires ~24% equity (300/(950+300)) and my Ace blocks some strong A‑x combos, so I have reasonable showdown/realization equity versus a wide BTN c‑bet range. Raising would be a polarized play I can’t credibly represent with this weak hand, and folding gives up too much with attractive pot odds. Plan: call to see the turn, keep the pot controlled OOP, fold to large turn aggression unless I pair my Ace or pick up a very favorable runout/check from villain; if I improve I’ll extract value, otherwise I’ll surrender to strong lines.

I paired my Ace on the turn (Ah6c on Kc 9d Jh Ad) — top pair with a weak kicker. I was OOP and called the flop to keep the pot manageable; now that I’ve improved I should extract value from worse A-x, Kx and single‑pair Jx hands and charge any float/draw hands, but avoid bloating the pot with a suspect kicker. Flash’s range (button open + flop c‑bet) contains a lot of Kx/Jx and some Ax; my Ah blocks a portion of his strongest Ax combos but two‑pair combos (AK, AJ, KJ, A9) remain possible. With my shortish effective stack (~16–17bb) I want a small‑to‑medium value bet rather than a big shove — half‑pot will get called by worse while keeping the pot controllable. Bet ~600 into 1250. If Flash check‑raises big or jams, I’ll fold; if he calls I’ll re‑evaluate the river for value or to fold to heavy aggression.

On the turn, the board reads Kc-9d-Jh-Ad. I hold 8d-7d, giving me a flush draw (7 outs: 2d, 3d, 4d, 5d, 6d, 10d, Qd). My previous assessment of having a gutshot was incorrect; with 7-8 in hand and 9-J-K-A on the board, there is no straight draw available. Minni, who is extremely short-stacked, has led for 600 into a pot of 1250. This donk bet after calling pre-flop and on the flop suggests she has improved, likely to a pair of Aces, two pair, or a straight (any Queen makes a straight). With only 7 outs to a non-nut flush and roughly 15% equity, calling the 600 is mathematically incorrect (needing ~24.5% for the immediate call, or more if she shoves her remaining 475 on the river). Given my substantial chip lead and the high likelihood that even if I hit my flush, it might not be the best hand or I won't get paid off by her tiny remaining stack, the most disciplined play is to fold.

Preflop on the button with 65 suited connectors, an excellent speculative hand in position with deep stacks. Unraised pot (pot=150 from blinds), wide raising range from button to steal blinds or play postflop profitably. Minni in SB is relatively short at ~29BB but still allows standard sizing. Raise to 3x BB (300 total) to apply pressure and isolate if called. Good playability for straights/flushes multiway or heads-up.

8♣3♠ is one of the worst starting hands — junk, easily dominated by a button open and the big blind’s defending range. I’m out of position for the whole hand (SB vs Button + BB), pot is 450 and it costs 250 to call, so I need ~35.7% equity to break even. 8-3o does not have that vs a button open and a likely defending BB, and a light 3‑bet bluff (raising to 500) is a risky line against deep/aggressive stacks that will often call or 4‑bet. With ~28.8bb effective, I should conserve chips for better spots. Fold and wait for a much stronger opportunity.

I am in the Big Blind with 9-6 offsuit. Elon, the Button, has opened for 3x. While I am getting decent pot odds to call (200 to win 450), 9-6 offsuit is a very weak hand that plays poorly out of position. Elon has been active and aggressive, and I would likely find myself in a difficult spot post-flop with middle-to-low cards. I will fold and wait for a more advantageous situation.

I’m on the button with 3♦7♣ — essentially junk. Pot is 150 and it’s 100 to call (min-raise to 200). My stack is ~28.7bb, but both opponents are deep and have shown they defend and 3-bet/call frequently (Elon especially). Calling 100 into a 150 pot requires roughly 40% equity to break even (100/(150+100)=40%), and 3‑7o has far less equity versus one or two defending ranges. Raising to try to steal could succeed occasionally but will often be met with calls or a 3-bet from these players, putting me in very unfavorable spots with a very weak hand. With position not enough to overcome the hand’s poor equity in multiway pots, the correct, low-variance play is to fold and conserve chips for better spots.

I am in the Small Blind with 7h 6d, a speculative suited connector-like hand. The Button (Minni) has folded. The pot is currently 150 (50 from my Small Blind and 100 from Elon's Big Blind). To complete the bet, I only need to add 50 more chips. Given that I am head-to-head against Elon and have a decent stack, calling to see a flop is a reasonable, low-cost play to potentially outplay the opponent or hit a strong draw, though a fold or a raise are also options. Given the small cost to see the flop, I will call.

Pocket Queens are a premium hand pre-flop, crushing the typical small blind limping range, which is often weak or speculative hands. Minni has folded, so it's heads-up against Flash, who has a large stack. Raising builds the pot for value, takes initiative, and potentially isolates or folds out weaker hands. A raise to 400 (4x BB) is standard sizing here: aggressive but not overcommitting given stacks (effective ~16k). Checking risks multi-way pots post-flop or giving free cards to draws.

I am in the Small Blind with 7h 6d. I initially called the Big Blind of 100, and now Elon has raised to 400. This means it costs me 300 more to call into a pot that would be 800 total. Although 7-6 offsuit is a speculative hand, the stack sizes are deep relative to the raise. However, I will be out of position for the rest of the hand against Elon, who has shown aggression in previous hands. Given the strength of my hand is marginal and I'll be playing from a positional disadvantage, folding is the most disciplined play to avoid a potentially expensive and difficult post-flop situation.

I am on the button with Jack-Five offsuit. The blinds are 50/100, and it is folded to me. While this hand is relatively weak, I am up against two opponents, one of whom (Minni) is extremely short-stacked with only about 27bb remaining. Raising here might induce a shove from Minni that I would have to fold to, or a call/re-raise from Elon. Given the stack dynamics and the weakness of my hand (J5o is not a standard opening hand from the button in most tight-aggressive ranges), folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my healthy stack of over 200bb.

92s is a weak hand with poor post-flop playability out of position against the BB. Minni has a short stack of 2775 (~28BB), but calling the 50 OOP risks tough spots with reverse implied odds on draws or weak pairs. Folding preserves stack for better spots in a long session aiming to maximize over 150 hands.

Preflop on the button with 6h4h, suited connectors which play well multiway in position. Pot is 150, to call only 100, offering excellent pot odds (1.5:1 direct, plus strong implied odds given stacks and position). Minni in SB is short-stacked at 2875 (~29BB), Flash deep in BB. Calling is cheap and allows seeing a flop with good playability postflop. Raising risks short-stack shove or isolation, but speculative hand prefers multiway pot. Folding too weak in position.

I’m in the small blind with K♦9♥ facing a limp from the button (Elon). That limp is likely a wide range — K9o has decent equity vs a limp but is vulnerable out of position if this stays multi-way. I want to take the initiative and either isolate the limp or pick up the pot preflop while keeping the pot manageable with a marginal hand. I’ll raise to 300 total (a standard ~3xBB isolation size). If I face a large 3-bet I’ll fold (hand is too marginal to gamble a big chunk of my ~28.8BB stack); if called, I’ll proceed cautiously out of position, looking to barrel for value on favorable K-high flops and to give up to heavy resistance on coordinated/ace-high boards.

I am in the Big Blind holding J9o. Elon (Button) limped, and Minni (Small Blind) raised to 300. Minni is very short-stacked with only 2625 chips remaining. J9o is a reasonable hand to defend against a small 3-bet squeeze from the blinds, especially getting 2.5:1 on a call. However, since Minni is so short, any post-flop action will likely result in her being all-in quickly. Given the pot odds and the fact that I am closing the action (unless Elon re-raises), calling is a standard play here to see a flop with a hand that has decent connectivity and high-card value in a 3-way pot.

On the button with 6h4h, suited connectors that play excellently multiway in position. Excellent pot odds: pot 700 to call 200 (3.5:1), plus strong implied odds against Minni's short stack (26BB) and Flash's deep stack. Limped initially for value, now facing standard raise from SB Minni who is aggressive but short. Calling keeps pot multiway, maximizes hand's playability on flush/straight draws. Raising risks Minni shoving her stack (easy call but variance), and isolates which isn't ideal for speculative hand. Folding too tight given position and price. Plan to play postflop aggressively if hit, control if miss.

I check.  Context: I raised preflop to 300 to isolate but both Elon (button) and Flash (BB) called, so this is a three‑way pot and I’m out of position. Board is A♣ Q♠ T♦ and my hand is K♦9♥ — no pair, no real backdoor flush and only a gutshot to a J (4 outs). Equity math: ~8.5% to hit on the turn and ~16% to hit by the river to make the Broadway straight; pairing the K adds a few percent but is often second‑best when an Ace or Queen is present. With two callers I have very little fold equity and betting here would frequently get called by better A/Q/T/Broadway holdings, so c‑betting is thin/negative EV. I’ll check to control the pot and see a free turn. Plan: if I face a small stab (~≤~200) I’ll consider a call for the pot odds to chase my 16% runout; I’ll fold to large bets/raises. If the turn brings a J or K I’ll shift to value/aggression; otherwise continue pot control and re‑evaluate vs aggression.

I have an open-ended straight draw on a board that likely connects with both opponents' ranges. Since Minni (the pre-flop aggressor) checked, I will check as well to see how Elon reacts. Checking allows me to potentially see a free turn card or evaluate the price if Elon decide to bet, keeping the pot manageable while I try to complete my draw.

Flop completely missed 6h4h: no pair, no straight/flush draw. Board Ac Qs Td connects well with Ax, broadway holdings likely in ranges. Both opponents checked, indicating possible capped ranges or traps, but multiway pot favors caution. Deep stack (162BB), position on button allows checking to control pot, realize any equity from live cards, and see turn for free (pot 900). Bluffing risks Minni's short stack (~26BB) shoving or Flash calling light; pure air poor spot to barrel without backdoors improving. Consistent with preflop plan: control when miss. Check to pot control and re-evaluate turn.

I raised preflop to isolate but both opponents called, so I'm out of position in a three‑way pot. I checked the flop as planned; the turn (7♦) doesn't improve my K♦9♥. My clean outs are 4 J's to make the Broadway straight and 3 K's to pair (≈7 outs, ~15% to improve on the river), and a paired K will often still be behind an Ace or better two‑pair. With two callers I have very little fold equity, so barreling the turn is thin / negative EV — a bet will usually get called by better hands or by hands that still beat a one‑pair K. I will control the pot and check to see the river; plan is to fold to substantial aggression and only continue to small river bets when the pot odds justify chasing my ~15% equity. If the river is a J or K I will shift to value/aggression; otherwise keep the pot small or concede the hand.

I have an open-ended straight draw with my J9 on a board of A-Q-T-7. Any King or Eight will give me a straight. The pre-flop aggressor, Minni, has checked both the flop and the turn, suggesting she might not have a strong hand or is playing a marginal one cautiously. Elon checked behind on the flop as well. By checking now, I can potentially see a free river card to try and hit my draw, or I can see what Elon does behind me. If Elon bets, I can calculate if I have the correct pot odds to call and chase the straight.

Button with complete air 6h4h on Qs Td Ac 7d board: no pair, no draws, minimal backdoor equity. Both opponents checked turn behind my flop check, suggesting capped ranges or weakness, but multiway pot from preflop limp/call favors caution over bluffing pure air. Minni short-stacked (~26BB) likely to shove wide if bet, Flash deep and called preflop raise so has some strength. Position allows free river card to realize any slim equity or induce bluffs. Bluffing risks high variance vs short stack shove or Flash hero-call; pot control aligns with speculative hand plan when missing. Checking maintains balance after limping pre, extracts thin value if somehow ahead, or caps range credibly.

I rivered top pair (K♦9♥ on Q♠ T♦ A♣ 7♦ K♥). Action checked to me after both opponents checked the flop and turn, which reduces the likelihood they have a strong Ace or two‑pair (those hands often bet earlier). My hand has thin but real value versus Qx/Tx/Jx/missed broadway holdings and is vulnerable to Ax or two‑pair. With two players in the hand I have limited fold equity, so a small thin value bet is appropriate to extract from worse pairs while keeping the pot under control. I’ll bet ~1/3 pot (300). If I face significant aggression (a large raise) I will fold—I’m only a single pair and don’t want to commit a big portion of my 2625 stack.

On the river, the board is Qs-Td-Ac-7d-Kh. My hole cards are J9, giving me a straight (Ten through Ace: T-J-Q-K-A). Although any Jack would have made a straight, the King on the river completed the high end. Minni has bet 300 into a pot of 900. Since I have a straight and only a specific J-X or X-J combination (which I hold one of) or a higher hand is unlikely given the action, I have a very strong hand. I will raise to extract value from Minni's remaining stack, as she has shown weakness by checking earlier and may be betting a marginal hand or a lower straight now that the King arrived.

River with complete air 6h4h on Qs Td Ac 7d Kh board. No pair, draw, or showdown value. Missed entirely despite suited connector playability earlier. Pot ~2100 to call 900 (~30% equity needed), but equity near 0% vs. Flash's check-raise range (Ax, sets, straights like QJ/TJ/KQ/A9 etc.). Minni's river bet after passivity suggests polarization; Flash's raise crushes. Bluff-catching impossible without equity or fold equity. Deep stack (162BB), fold preserves chips for premium spots over 50+ hands left. Consistent with pot control plan when missing.

I bet a small 300 on the river for thin value and got raised to 900 by Flash, so I must call 600. Pot before the river action was 2,100; after the raise the effective pot if I call will be ~3,600, so I need ~16.7% equity to break even. My K9 only makes a single king — it loses to any Ace, any two‑pair (AK, KQ, QT, etc.) and the few straights (J9). Flash checked the flop and turn and then raised the river over a small bet, which is a polarizing line that is much more often value (Ax, two pair, straights) than a bluff on this dry board. There were very few bluffs possible (no real flush draws and limited straight combos), so his bluff frequency is likely low. Calling 600 (~26% of my 2,325 stack) for marginal equity is not profitable long‑term, and raising would be thin and risky. I’ll fold and preserve my stack for better spots.

I'm on the button with K♦4♠ and 23.25bb (stack 2325). Vs the blinds this is a marginal but stealable spot — I have position and can open a standard 3bb raise to pick up the pot or isolate one defender. A min-raise to 200 is too small (invites too many callers); an all‑in is unnecessary and will get called too often by these deep-stacked opponents. I will raise to 300 (3bb). Plan: if I face a large 3‑bet or shove I will fold K4o; if I'm called I'll play postflop aggressively in position on favorable textures and otherwise control the pot.

I am in the Small Blind with 5s 4d, a very weak offsuit hand. Minni has raised to 300 from the Button. Given my poor hand strength and the fact that I would be out of position for the rest of the hand, there is no reason to continue. Folding is the standard and most profitable play here to preserve my stack.

KsJs is a premium suited Broadway hand with excellent playability, straight and flush potential, and domination potential against button raising ranges. Minni's button raise after SB fold is likely wide, including many hands we dominate or have equity against. Pot odds to call are good (200 into 450), but 3-betting for value and fold equity is optimal OOP. Standard sizing vs button open is 3x the raise (900 total). Minni's stack (2025) is covered, allowing postflop play if called. History shows Minni raises frequently but folds to aggression often. This maximizes EV by isolating or winning preflop.

I opened to 3bb with K♦4♠ as a button steal, but Elon’s 3‑bet to 900 is a standard, value‑heavy sizing that represents a strong range (pocket pairs, AK/AQ, strong Kx and broadways). I must call 600 more to win a 1,250 pot — I’m getting roughly 32% equity (600/(1250+600)), which K4o almost never has vs a typical 3‑bet range and is frequently dominated. A 4‑bet shove as a bluff is possible with a K blocker, but versus Elon’s stack and aggression it’s high variance and will be called by too many better hands. With only ~20bb effective I’m preserving chips for better spots rather than spewing off equity with a dominated hand. Fold.

I am on the button with 5c2h. This is a very weak hand. Even though Minni is extremely short-stacked (less than 20 big blinds), I have no reason to enter the pot with such a poor hand. Folding is the standard and correct play here.

3-handed pre-flop, Button (Flash) folded UTG. I'm SB with JQo offsuit, a strong hand in this spot vs BB. Minni in BB has short stack (1925, ~19BB), likely to fold weak hands or shove wide. Raising takes initiative, applies fold equity to steal the 150 pot dead money, and JQo has good equity vs her calling/shoving range (blocks strong hands, plays well post-flop). OOP but heads-up preferred with initiative. Standard sizing 3x BB to 300 total. If jammed on, pot odds favorable (~30% needed, have 40%+ vs loose jam range). Limping too passive, folding too tight.

Facing Elon's 3x raise to 300 from the SB, pot is 400 and I must call 200 (total commit 300). My stack is ~19 BB — not so short that I must shove, and not deep enough to be speculative with marginal hands. T6s has decent equity vs a likely wide SB opening (backdoor spade/straight potential and some blocker value), and calling gives me ~33% pot-odds to see a flop. A min‑raise to 500 is too small to earn meaningful fold equity and a shove is too high-variance with this hand against a deep, aggressive opener. I'll defend and call to realize equity and evaluate on the flop.

Flop is 2c-8c-3c, a highly coordinated monochromatic board favoring Minni's BB calling range which includes many clubs, suited connectors, and pairs. My JdQs completely whiffs: no pair, no flush draw (one spade), no straight draw. Pure air with marginal overcards and poor playability OOP. Preflop raise was standard for initiative and fold equity, but now initiative lost with a miss. Betting risks her shoving any equity/value (~3x pot with her 1725 stack commits her), and I have no fold equity vs short stack likely to continue wide. Calling a bet impossible without hand. Check to realize equity on missed bluffs/check-backs, control pot, and avoid spew. Deep stacked, preserve chips for better spots over remaining 49 hands.

Flop: 2c 8c 3c — a wet, monotone clubs board. My hand T♠6♠ has no club, no pair, and essentially no realistic draw (only 6 outs to pair; ~27–28% to improve by river). Elon was the preflop raiser but checked the flop — this can be either weakness or a slowplay with a club/monster. Leading out as a bluff has very little fold equity against a club-heavy raising range and would commit chips with poor/raw equity. With ~17 BB effective and a 600-chip pot, the lowest-variance, +EV line is to check and control the pot: see a turn for free, fold to a significant bet, and only call small probes where pot odds justify chasing pair-outs. Therefore I check now.

Turn Js pairs my Jd for top pair with Q kicker on a scary monochromatic club flop that heavily favors Minni's BB defending range (clubs, suited connectors, small pairs). She checked flop behind my check, possibly weak or trapping. Pot 600, her effective stack 1725 (~2.8x pot), deep OOP with improved but marginal top pair vulnerable to flushes, straights (e.g., 45), sets, two-pair in her range. Betting risks check-raise/jam from better hands or draws, poor implied odds; small bet gets thin value but no fold equity vs short stack. Checking controls pot OOP, induces bluffs from missed draws on river, realizes equity vs her weak checks, preserves stack for better spots in 49 remaining hands. Free river cheap; unlikely to improve her much without committing.

I missed — I have T♠6♠ on 2♣-8♣-3♣-J♠. Elon was the preflop raiser and has checked flop and turn, which makes his range ambiguous (could be a slow-played club, a medium pair, or air). I have no clubs and only ~6 outs (~13%) to pair by the river, so I have poor equity and very little reliable fold equity if I lead. With ~17 BB effective and a 600-chip pot, betting here is high variance and likely to be called by hands that beat me (clubs, any pair) or fold only weaker hands where I’m not extracting value. Lowest-variance +EV line is to check and control the pot: see the river for free and fold to a significant bet unless I pair or pick up a take-it card. Therefore I check behind on the turn.

River 4h is a relatively blank card that doesn't complete obvious new draws beyond the existing flush possibilities (which were already there) or obscure straights. I paired my Jack on the turn for top pair with Queen kicker, a decent but vulnerable hand on this coordinated club-heavy board (possible flushes, sets like 22/33/88, two-pairs like 28/23/24). Minni defended wide from BB preflop (suited connectors, clubs, small pairs, Ax suited) but checked flop and turn OOP, suggesting she missed her equity-heavy range (no strong flush draw or made hand that wants to bet), weak pairs, or trapping monster. Pot 600, her stack 1725 (~2.9x pot). Consistent with turn plan: checking induces bluffs from her missed draws/clubs (high frequency from BB def range), realizes equity OOP without risking stack vs potential check-raise/shove from better hands. Value betting thin risks folding out worse (e.g., 8x, Ax, gutshots) while getting raised by flushes/sets; showdown value strong vs checked-back range. Deep stacked myself (16250), preserve for 49 hands left; let her bluff or check back worse. Optimal check.

Elon (preflop raiser) checked flop, turn and now the river on a monotone 2♣-8♣-3♣-J♠-4♥ board. I hold T♠6♠ — no club, no pair, essentially very little showdown equity. Checking here surrenders the pot to any hand that actually has a club or a pair; a small value-polarized probe can often fold out A/K-high and other unpaired hands that checked back. Pot is 600 and my stack is ~1,725 (~17 BB). A modest one‑third pot bet (200) applies pressure while keeping my commitment reasonable if called. With realistic assumptions about Elon's checking range a ~30–40% fold frequency makes this sized bluff +EV; if he calls I can rarely win but the cost is limited. I'll make a small exploitative bluff to try to take the pot.

River board completes no new obvious draws beyond the existing club flush. Holding top pair top kicker (paired turn Jack) after checking through, Minni's small 200 bet into 600 pot (1/3 pot) from BB OOP is polarized: likely bluffs from missed flush draws (heavy in BB def range like KcTc, Qc9c, suited connectors without flush), thin value (e.g., 8x, weaker Jx, A-high no club), or monsters trapping (sets, two-pair) but unlikely given check-check lines. Her stack ~1525 effective post-bet, pot odds 4:1 require only ~20% equity which top pair crushes vs bluffs/weak pairs and has live equity vs better. Folding spews value; raising risks folding out worse or facing jam from nuts OOP with deep stack. Cheap call realizes full equity, consistent with pre-turn plan to control pot OOP and induce bluffs. Preserve stack for 49 hands left.

Pocket eights is a strong premium hand preflop, well ahead of the limping/calling ranges from blinds. On the button, I have excellent position postflop against both opponents. Minni in SB is short-stacked (~14.75BB), so raising can induce a shove from her lighter range which I can call profitably, or take it down if they fold. BB Flash is deep but position favors me. Standard raise size of 3x the BB to 300 builds the pot for value while denying equity to draws later. Limping wastes position; folding is too tight.

Facing a BTN open to 300 from the small blind with 6♥8♦ (offsuit), I'm out of position with a weak, poorly-connected hand and only ~14.8bb effective (stack 1,475). Calling 250 (to 300 total) gives break-even equity ~250/(450+250)=~36% versus the raiser’s range — my hand’s equity is likely below that, and the big blind still has the option to act, increasing multiway risk. A min 3-bet to 500 is too small to fold out better hands and leaves me in difficult postflop spots; shoving my stack with 6-8o is too loose vs this opener and the deep callers. Preserving my ~15bb and waiting for a better spot (in position or with a stronger hand) is higher EV than calling or risking a marginal shove here, so I fold.

I am in the Big Blind with T2o. Elon, on the button, has raised to 300. This is a standard 3x open. Looking at my hand, T2o is extremely weak and plays very poorly out of position. Minni has already folded from the small blind. While I have a large stack, there is no strategic reason to defend this specific hand against a button open from Elon, who has been relatively active and aggressive. I will fold and wait for a better spot to utilize my chip lead.

I’m short-stacked (1475 ≈ 14.8BB) on the button with Q♦7♦. With under ~15BB the correct strategy is to be aggressive preflop — either shove or fold — rather than limp/call or make a small raise and get squeezed. Q7s on the button is a reasonable shove: it has blocker value to Qx, suited equity, and decent fold equity against two blind players (Flash SB and Elon BB) who are deep but frequently aggressive. Calling 100 would leave me in a marginal multiway pot with ~13.8BB behind; a small raise invites a 3-bet and leaves me in an awkward spot. Shoving maximizes my fold equity to pick up the 150 pot immediately or isolates a single caller where I have showdown chances to double up. I’ll shove my full stack to maximize EV.

Minni has raised to 1475, which appears to be an all-in move given her stack is now 0. I am in the Small Blind with Jack-Nine suited (Jh 9h). The pot currently contains 1625 chips (1475 from Minni, 100 from Elon, and my 50 SB). To call, I need to commit an additional 1425 chips. This gives me pot odds of approximately 1.14:1, requiring about 46.7% equity to break even. Against a standard Button shoving range of approximately 40-50% of hands, Jack-Nine suited holds roughly 48-50% equity. Given the strength of my hand relative to a wide button-shove range and the fact that I have a very healthy stack of over 21,000, this is a profitable call. I will call rather than raise to see if Elon (the Big Blind) wants to come along with a wider range or if he folds, allowing me to go heads-up against the short stack.

87o is a marginal speculative hand at best, but offsuit with only a one-gap connection, poor playability postflop especially out of position multiway. Facing a large button raise to 14.75BB from Minni (who raises frequently but this size suggests strength or isolation) and a flat call from Flash in SB, their combined range is strong and likely to dominate mine (e.g., Ax suited, pairs, broadways). Pot odds are about 2.2:1 (1375 to call into 3050), insufficient for the equity needed (~31%) against this action. Folding preserves my deep 171x BB stack for better spots in 150-hand game.

I am on the button with King-Six suited, which is a strong starting hand for three-handed play. My goal is to open for a standard raise of 300 to put pressure on the blinds and take control of the pot. Minni is quite short-stacked, so I must be prepared for a potential shove from her, but the equity of K6s is generally high enough to call a short-stack jam if necessary.

J5o is a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability, especially out of position in the small blind facing a button raise from Flash, who has been aggressive in late position throughout the history. Minni is short-stacked in the BB but doesn't change my decision. Pot odds of calling 250 into 450 (~36% equity needed) are poor given my hand's low equity against Flash's wide but strong button opening range (likely 25-40% hands). Post-flop OOP, this hand struggles to realize equity. Folding preserves my deep stack (170BB) for better spots in this long cash game session aiming to maximize stack over 150 hands.

Flash opened to 300 from the button. I’m in the big blind with K♣8♦ and ~29.5bb effective. Calling the extra 200 (300 total) is the best line: the pot is 450, so calling 200 gives me ~30.8% pot odds — K8o has roughly that equity versus a standard button opening range, so defending is +EV. A min 3-bet to 500 is too small to be an effective bluff (won’t fold out enough and often leaves me dominated), and shoving is overly polar and unnecessary with this holding. Folding is too tight — we’re getting good odds to see a flop and can control the hand postflop. Plan: call to 300 total, proceed cautiously on dry, highly aggressive boards and continue/raise on favorable flops (top pair, strong draws) or check/fold to heavy action when missed.

Check. I defended preflop to see a cheap flop — this board (9♣-5♣-3♦) is very dry except for two clubs and I only have K♣8♦: one overcard and a single club blocker but no real draw. Out of position vs a wide button range, I want to control pot size and see what Flash does rather than barrel into a spot I can be exploited in. Plan: check and call modest continuation bets (roughly up to ~1/2-pot, ~300–325) because I have decent equity vs a wide c-betting range; fold to large pot-sized c-bets or raises that denote strong made hands/draws. If Flash checks back I’ll take the turn for free and only continue if I improve or pick up clear fold equity.

On the flop of 5c 3d 9c, I have King-high. My opponent, Minni, has checked to me. This flop is relatively disconnected and favors my pre-flop raising range.
