Given that Minni is playing a short stack, I want to place a continuation bet to capitalize on any fold equity and protect my hand's value against potential draws. A bet of around half the pot (300) is standard here to apply pressure while keeping the price manageable.

Flash (BTN) c-bet 300 into a 950 pot on 5c-3d-9c. I’m OOP with K♣8♦ — no pair, a single club blocker and only backdoor club/straight outs. Calling 300 into 950 requires ~24% equity (300/(950+300)≈24%), and vs a wide button c-betting range K8o has roughly that equity plus decent implied odds to hit a K or a club. A raise here would be too thin and polarizing (I’m weak and can be easily exploited), and folding is too tight given the price. Plan: call 300 to see the turn and re-evaluate. If I pair the King or pick up a meaningful club draw on the turn I can apply pressure or extract value; if the turn is a blank and Flash barrels big, I’ll fold to heavy aggression.

Turn analysis: the 6♣ gives me a four-flush (K♣ plus 9♣-5♣-6♣) and there are three kings left — so 9 club outs + 3 king outs = 12 outs ≈ 12/46 ≈ 26% to improve on the river. Pot is 1,250 and my stack is 2,450 (~24.5bb). I am out of position and currently have only a draw and K-high; betting now as a semi-bluff would risk a larger commitment out of position against a very deep, aggressive opponent and won’t fold out enough of his calling range to make it clearly +EV. A raise is too polar and a shove is unnecessary with only a draw. Checking preserves pot control and lets me realize equity for cheap. Plan: check the turn. If Flash checks back I’ll see the river for free and value-bet or bluff-shovel appropriately if I hit a club or pair; if he bets the river small (roughly ≤ ~650) I’ll call to realize my ~26% equity, but I’ll fold to pot-sized or larger river bets which price me incorrectly and indicate strong made hands.

On the turn, I have improved to a pair of sixes. However, the 6c also completes a possible club flush and doesn't change the fact that any 9 still beats me. Minni is playing a very short stack, and her check could be an attempt to check-raise or simply a realization of the board's increased danger. By checking back, I control the size of the pot, avoid being forced to make a decision against a potential shove, and look to realize my showdown value on the river.

I missed my turn draw — the 6♣ gave me a four‑card club draw but the river J♠ does not complete it. I have only K‑high with no pair and no showdown value. Pot is 1,250 and my stack ~2,450; bluffing out of position here risks a meaningful portion of my stack and would need a very high fold frequency (betting ~600 into 1,250 needs ~32%+ folds) which I don’t expect from Flash given his flop aggression and turn check (range includes many pairs and medium-strength hands that will call a river bluff). Optimal, low-variance line is to check and fold to a bet — if Flash checks back we see a cheap showdown (muck K‑high), if he bets I’ll fold to standard sizes. Action: check.

On the river, the board is 5c-3d-9c-6c-Js. I have a pair of sixes. Minni has checked for the third time this hand. Given the presence of three clubs and multiple overcards to my pair (9, J), my hand has some showdown value but is not strong enough for a value bet. Checking back allows me to reach showdown for free and win against her missed draws or smaller pairs, while avoiding a potential check-raise from a short stack who might have a 9, a Jack, or a flush.

Pocket Jacks is a premium hand, top 5% preflop. On the button with position on both opponents, especially short-stacked Minni (24BB) in SB and deep Flash in BB. Pot is 150, to call 100 (likely a limp or complete). Standard play is to raise for value, to isolate Minni's wide range or build pot vs Flash. Raising to 4x the BB (400 total) is aggressive but balanced, denying equity to worse hands and preparing for potential shove from Minni which JJ crushes. Effective stack with Minni is 2400, so sizing well under that to induce action.

Facing a button open to 400 with the big blind still to act. I’m in the small blind with 8♥6♣ — a weak, unsuited, poorly-connected hand that plays terribly out of position. To call 350 more to 400 would put me in multiway/out-of-position pots with little equity and poor post‑flop playability. A light 3‑bet is possible vs a wide BTN steal, but this hand has weak blockers and will often get called or 4‑bet, making it a suboptimal bluff. Stack is only 24bb — preserve chips for spots with better equity or blockers. Fold and wait for a better opportunity.

I am in the Big Blind with Ten of Clubs and Four of Diamonds. The Button (Elon) has raised to 400. Although I am getting decent pot odds to call (300 to win a total pot of 850), T4o is an extremely weak hand to play out of position. Elon has been active and aggressive, and I would likely find myself in a difficult spot on most flops. Given Minni's short stack and the fact that I want to maintain my chip lead, there is no reason to defend with such a marginal hand. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

I'm on the Button with K♠5♠ and 24bb (2400). With two players left to act (SB and BB) this is a standard spot to open-raise for fold equity and to leverage position — K5s has enough playability (suited King, backdoor draws) to be in my opening range. The pot is 150 and it costs 100 to see a flop, but raising to 300 total (standard 3x-ish BB open on this table) applies pressure, follows the table's common sizing, and leaves me comfortable postflop room to maneuver. Plan: if called I'll c-bet favorable textures and play position; if I face a large 3-bet (e.g. ~900+), K5s is usually a fold given my stack and equity; a small 3-bet might be considered depending on the villain's tendencies. Overall raising to 300 maximizes my long-term EV here.

I am in the Small Blind with 9s 2d, a very weak hand. Minni, on the button, has raised to 300. Although the price to call is relatively small, 92 offsuit has extremely poor playability out of position and low equity against an opening range. Given the strength of the hand and the positional disadvantage, folding is the most prudent play.

Pocket Jacks are a premium hand facing a button raise, which is likely a wide range including many suited connectors, Ax, broadway, etc. Minni has a short stack of 2100, so 3-betting isolates her and builds the pot with value. Folding the SB leaves us heads-up. A standard 3-bet size of 900 (3x her raise) applies pressure while leaving her with decisions post-flop if she continues. Calling is too passive and allows multi-way pots or free plays.

I opened to 300 on the button with K♠5♠ (stack 2100). Elon (BB) 3-bet to 900; pot is 1250 and it costs me 600 to call (~32% pot odds). Calling would leave me 1500 behind (SPR ≈0.8) — with K5s I’m heavily dominated by a typical 3-bet range (JJ+/AK/AQs/KQs) and my raw equity is well below the needed ~32%. A 4-bet shove as a bluff is thin here: Elon’s sizing and table history suggest a value-heavy range that will call or have strong equity, so the shove is high risk with low EV. With 21bb and many hands left in the session the correct, risk-managed play is to fold and wait for a better spot.

I am on the button with 9-7 offsuit. In a three-handed game, opening ranges are wider, but 9-7 offsuit is still a relatively weak hand. More importantly, Minni in the Big Blind is short-stacked with only 2,000 chips (20 BB). Short stacks are prone to shoving over button raises, and 9-7 offsuit does not have enough equity to call a 20 BB shove. To avoid committing chips to a marginal spot against a short stack and a deep-stacked Elon in the Small Blind, folding is the most disciplined play.

In the small blind facing only the big blind after button folds, heads-up against Minni who is short-stacked at 20BB (2000 chips). My 2c5c is a suited connector with good playability for straights and flushes. Pot is 150 (my SB 50 + BB 100), calling 50 more gives excellent pot odds (3:1) and the implied odds are strong given my deep stack (175BB). Position is OOP post-flop, but the cost is minimal (0.3BB), and the hand performs well multi-street against a short stack's wide range. Folding wastes the SB without upside; raising is too strong for this marginal hand. Standard play is to complete.

Td8s offsuit is a marginal hand and I only have ~20BB. Elon just limped from the small blind; raising to isolate would commit a meaningful chunk of my short stack and it doesn’t change my post‑flop position (he’s the dealer and will have last action). Better to keep the pot small and realize equity. I’ll check and see a flop; fold to sizable aggression unless I pair up or pick up a strong draw, and only call small bets where my odds justify it. If he checks back I can proceed cautiously on favorable turns.

Flop of 4s4h Qd gives me a weak gutshot straight draw (4 outs to a 3 for the wheel straight) with my 2c5c, plus negligible backdoor flush and straight potential since no flush draw currently (only two clubs). No pair, no strong draw against a paired board with a queen kicker. Pot is 200, I'm OOP first to act against short-stacked Minni (2000 chips, 10BB effective now). Betting risks getting shoved on by her wide checking range, and my hand has poor equity against her shoving range. Checking realizes equity for free on the turn, controls the pot with a marginal draw, and keeps me in position to fold to aggression or continue if board improves. Bluffing here is low EV vs short stack who can cheaply call or jam.

Check. I have T8o on 4♠4♥Q♦ — no pair and only ~6 clean outs (3 tens + 3 eights), roughly ~24% to hit by the river. With a 20BB stack I want to keep the pot small and realize equity rather than build a pot I can’t comfortably commit to. Elon limped and checked, so betting here would have little fold equity and would commit a meaningful chunk of my stack. I’ll check and fold to sizable aggression; I’ll only call very small bets that give correct pot odds (e.g. a bet around 50), and re-evaluate on the turn if I pair up or pick up a strong draw.

Board is paired 4s with Q and K, very dry. My 2c5c has no pair, no strong draw (backdoor club flush and gutshot at best, but effectively air). Previously checked flop to realize equity OOP vs Minni's weak checking range after limping BB. Turn Kh doesn't improve me and adds possible Kx value to her range, but she checked flop suggesting weakness. Pot 200 small, her 2000 stack (10BB effective). Betting is a bluff with fold equity but OOP risks her jamming wide (including better bluffs or traps), committing me deep-stacked. Checking controls pot, induces her bluffs, takes free river card where clubs hit ~9% or runner-runner straight/flush possible. High EV to check down air vs likely weak range, avoiding reverse SPEW.

Board is 4♠ 4♥ Q♦ K♥, my hand Td8s, pot 200, my stack 2000 (≈20BB). Elon limped preflop and has shown a very passive line (checked flop and turn). I have only 6 outs to improve on the river (3 tens + 3 eights) — 6/46 ≈ 13% equity on the river. The table’s minimum bet on this street is 100, which prevents a tiny probe bet: a 100-chip bet would need to fold >100/(200+100)=33% of the time to be +EV as a bluff, and I would need >33% equity to call such a bet — I only have ≈13%. Given my shortish stack and the line shown by the opponent, I should avoid building the pot with a thin equity hand that is unlikely to improve. Best line is pot control: check and realize equity to the river. Contingency plan: if Elon bets the river very small (≈≤30 chips, break-even threshold ≈30), call; fold to any meaningful bet; if I pair or improve on the river I can pursue value then.

River board 4s4h Qd Kh 9c with 2c5c is pure air: no pair, no completed draw, worst possible hand. Pot 200, OOP vs Minni's 2000 stack (~10BB effective). Both checked flop and turn, capping ranges to showdown value like Ax, low-medium pairs (below Q/K), or traps (44/QK). Bluffing OOP on river in checked-through pot has low fold equity against her wide passive range after multiple checks—she calls stations light with any piece (Q+,K+,9+,A-high, paired 4s unlikely). Small bet (e.g., 100-200) denied odds poorly vs short stack who cheaply calls/offers implied reverse; larger bet risks jam/call wide. Consistent with prior streets' plan to check down marginal/air OOP vs weak BB limper, realize 0 equity for free at showdown (lose anyway) or fold to her thin value bet, avoiding -EV bluff into capped range. No blockers to value, poor story after checking twice. Check maximizes EV by pot control.

Pot is 200, my stack ~2000 (≈20BB). I hold Td8s on 4♠ 4♥ Q♦ K♥ 9♣ — at the river I have no outs and essentially no showdown-improving equity. Elon limp‑checked preflop and then checked flop/turn/river, a very passive line that makes it unlikely he will fold to a small bluff often enough to make a bet +EV. The minimum meaningful bet here is 100, which would require ~33% equity to call profitably (100/(200+100)) — I have ~0% — so calling is incorrect. Betting 100 as a bluff would also require >33% fold frequency to be +EV, which I don't expect from his line. Therefore I will control the pot and take the free showdown: check. If he fires any river bet (≥100) I will fold.

Preflop on the button with TsJd, a decent speculative hand with straight potential and good position. Pot is 150 with only 100 to call, offering excellent implied odds if we hit. Minni in SB is short-stacked at 21.5BB and may have limped or min-raised weakly; Flash in BB is deep. Raising takes the initiative, applies pressure on the short stack who might fold or shove (my hand has good equity vs shoving range from SB), and charges the BB. Standard play is to raise to 3x the BB or current bet size. Min raise is 200, but 300 is a good size to build the pot or take it down immediately. Folding too weak here; flat calling is passive OOP for opponents.

I have 2♦7♣ — the absolute worst starting hand. Elon opened to 300 from the button; I’m in the small blind and Flash (BB) is still to act. Calling 250 (300 total) out of position is a losing play: the pot is 450 so I’d need ~36% equity to break even, and 2-7o has far less versus a button opening range. Three-betting or shoving as a bluff is also a poor choice here — I have almost no fold equity and would be in bad shape if called. With a 21.5BB stack I should preserve chips for better spots. Fold preflop.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Six offsuit. Elon has raised to 300 from the Button, which is a standard opening move. With Minni already folding the Small Blind, the action is on me. A6o is a solid hand to defend the Big Blind with against a wide Button opening range, especially given the pot odds (calling 200 to win a total pot of 650). 3-betting is an option, but calling allows me to see a flop and realize my hand's equity without bloating the pot out of position against an aggressive opponent.

I have flopped trips with an Ace kicker on a 6-5-6 board. This is a very strong hand. Since Elon was the pre-flop aggressor, I will check to him to allow him to continuation-bet with his air or marginal hands, which he might fold if I lead out. This maximizes my chances of extracting value later in the hand.

Heads-up on the flop with TsJd on a paired 6h-5s-6d board. No pair, no strong draws, just ten-high with minimal backdoor equity. Flash checked from the BB after calling my button raise preflop, which often indicates weakness on this dry, paired board where he might have missed (overcards, weak pairs, or total air). My button range is strong and hits this board decently (A6, 65, 76, sets, etc.), so a continuation bet exploits his checking range and takes down the pot frequently. Pot is 650; a half-pot bet of 325 builds it while risking little relative to stacks (deep: me 17k, him 20k). If called, re-eval turn in position; fold to resistance or check/fold later. Checking back risks giving free cards to draws or letting him realize equity, and misses value from folds.

I have flopped trips with an Ace kicker on a 6h-5s-6d board. Elon, the pre-flop aggressor, has followed up with a continuation bet of 325 into a 650 pot. My hand is extremely strong, but raising here might force out his bluffs or marginal hands like overcards (AK, AQ) or small pairs. By calling, I keep his entire range in and can look to extract more value on the turn or river when the board texture develops or if he decides to fire a second barrel. This also protects my calling range in this spot.

I have trips with an Ace kicker on a 6h-5s-6d-8d board. After check-calling the flop to keep Elon's range wide, I will check again on the turn. This allows Elon, who was the pre-flop and flop aggressor, the opportunity to continue betting with his bluffs, overcards, or overpairs. Leading out now might cause him to fold his weaker hands, whereas checking maximizes the potential to extract more value or trap him if he decides to fire a second barrel.

Turn board 8d brings a diamond flush draw possibility but doesn't complete strong draws for Flash's likely calling range from BB (weak pairs, overcards, gutshots). I have TsJd: overcards with backdoor potential but no made hand or strong draw. However, in position after Flash's second check, my button c-bet range continues to look strong (trips with 6x, two pair like 65/56/68, sets, overpairs). Flash's check-call flop then check suggests capping range at medium strength or weakness; he folds too often to a second barrel. Pot 1300, deep stacks (me 16.8k, him 19.7k). Half-pot bet of 650 pressures folds from air/small pairs while risking manageable amount. Checking risks giving free river card to draws/improvers or multiway pot value misread; betting maximizes fold equity and builds pot for premium hands in range. Standard continuation in position to deny equity and exploit his range.

I have trips with an Ace kicker on a 6h-5s-6d-8d board. This is a very strong hand, though the 8d does complete a possible straight for a hand like 79. Elon has been the aggressor throughout the hand, betting both the flop and the turn. If I raise now, I risk folding out his bluffs (like overcards or missed draws) or marginal hands (like mid-pairs) that might continue betting on the river. By calling, I keep his entire range in play and can potentially extract more value on the river, while also controlling the pot size in case he has a made straight or a full house.

I have successfully improved to a full house (Aces full of Sixes) on the river. Throughout the hand, I have check-called to keep Elon's range wide and allow him to continue with his aggressive betting lead. On the river, checking for a third time is the most effective way to extract maximum value. This play induces bluffs from his missed straight draws (like 74 or 79) and allows him to value bet hands like a straight, a trip six with a lower kicker, or a lower two-pair. If Elon bets, I plan to check-raise to capitalize on the strength of my hand. Leading out now (donking) might cause him to fold his bluffs and would likely only be called by hands that would have bet anyway.

River Ad completes the diamond flush draw and brings an Ace, which polarizes Flash's range further—he likely either improved to top pair/good kicker, a flush, or a straight (like 47, 79 for wheel or higher), or he has a stubborn medium strength hand like a 6x or 7x that check-called flop/turn but now checks facing potential strength. My TsJd is pure bluff—no pair, no flush, no straight (board straights possible around it but I miss), just Jack-high with some backdoors that didn't hit. I've represented a strong range (trips 6s, two-pair combos like 65/56/68/58/A6s, sets, overpairs) through double-barreling in position, and Flash's triple check (flop/turn/river) caps him at likely non-nutted hands—he'd lead or raise nutted stuff more often vs my aggression. Pot 2600 deep (~6BB), betting ~1000-1300 could get folds from 6x, small pairs, missed draws (7x, overcards), and his history shows he folds rivers after check-calls (e.g., Hand 10,12,21,36, etc.). Fold equity ~50-60% vs his capped range; if called, I lose, but EV+ bluff sizing half-pot exploits his passivity. Checking risks him having showdown value (Ax,77+ beat me) and misses bluff value now that no more cards. Thin value unlikely (Ad might cap), so bluff to maximize pressure.

I have rivered a full house (Sixes full of Aces) on a board of 6h-5s-6d-8d-Ad. Throughout the hand, I have check-called Elon's bets to keep his range wide and induce bluffs or value bets with inferior hands. The river Ace is an excellent card as it significantly strengthens my hand while also potentially improving Elon to trips or two-pair if he holds an Ace. Given Elon has triple-barreled, he likely has a strong hand or a determined bluff. I will now raise for value, as I beat all straights, all trip sixes with lower kickers, and all two-pair combinations. While AA, 88, and 55 technically beat me, my holding makes AA extremely unlikely, and a raise to 3900 total aims to extract maximum value from the rest of his range.

Facing a river check-raise to 3900 (2600 more) into an 7800 pot after my triple-barrel bluff line with TsJd (jack-high, no pair, no flush, no straight). Board 6h5s6d8dAd features paired sixes, flush completion with three diamonds, and possible straights like 47 (45678). My range represented strong hands like sets/trips (66), two-pairs (56/65/68/A6), overpairs, but Flash's check-call flop/turn then check-raise river screams value: likely two diamonds for flush (beats most of my range except nut flush combos I rarely have), Ax top pair, or 47 straight. BB calling preflop vs button raise includes suited connectors/diamonds that hit hard here. Pot odds ~3:1 (7800/2600, need 25% equity), but TsJd has 0% vs value range and minimal fold equity realization already exploited. Flash's history (e.g., Hand 77 big river bet value, Hand 83 calls value, folds to aggression when weak) suggests he doesn't bluff-raise rivers lightly after passivity. Calling spews chips deep-stacked; fold preserves 14.8k stack for superior edges in 42 hands left. Standard discipline: bluff caught, exit.

I'm on the button with Ad9c (A9o) and a 2,150 stack (~21.5BB). Pot is 150 and it costs 100 to see the flop. This is a standard spot to seize initiative — A9o has good blockers and postflop playability in position. I will open-raise to 300 (≈3x BB / standard sizing here) to get fold equity and isolate the blinds. This size is big enough to fold out weaker holdings but preserves chips if I face a 3-bet. Plan: if called, play in position — c-bet favorable A- or dry flops, check/control on coordinated boards; if facing a large 3-bet (>~800–900) I will usually fold to avoid getting dominated or committing most of my stack.

I am in the Small Blind with K4o. Minni, who has a very short stack of 1850 total, has raised to 300 from the Button. Folding is a very reasonable option given the weak hand and being out of position, but K-high has some equity. However, I want to avoid committing chips to a pot where I'll be out of position for the rest of the hand with a marginal holding against a player who is likely looking to commit their remaining stack soon. Raising is unnecessary here. Calling might be okay, but folding preserves my stack and avoids a potentially tricky post-flop situation against a short stack.

Hand is 2d5c offsuit, one of the worst possible starting hands with minimal playability or equity against a button raise range. Minni raised to 300 from button after SB fold; she has been aggressive in prior hands but even wide ranges crush this. Pot odds ~2.25:1 for calling 200 more, but equity too low (~20-30% max vs loose range), and position out of position post-flop. Short stack on Minni (1850) doesn't change folding trash. Preserve stack for better spots.

I am on the button with 8s 2h, a very weak hand. Minni, in the big blind, has an extremely short stack (2,200 chips, 22 big blinds). Elon is in the small blind. While I could attempt to steal the blinds, 82o is too weak to play profitably in this spot, especially with the risk of being jammed on by the short stack. I will fold.

57o is a trash hand: offsuit, gapped, poor post-flop playability. From SB, completing puts me OOP vs BB's wide range. Pot odds 3:1 insufficient given low equity (~30-40% vs random) and Minni's short stack (2200) limits implied odds. Minni may check/fold or raise weak hands. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots. Goal is long-term stack maximization over 150 hands.

3-handed on the button with 7c9c suited connectors, deep effective stack vs BB Flash (very deep) after SB Minni (short 23BB) likely folded. Pot 150 (blinds), to call 100 for a limp. Excellent spot: 79s is a strong speculative hand in position with great playability, straight/flush potential, and domination avoidance. BB checked, so raise to take initiative, build pot when ahead, fold equity vs calling range, and realize equity postflop in position if called. Standard 3x open to 300 (3BB sizing) balances steal/value. Minni short so even if she wakes up unlikely profitable defend. Maximizes EV long-term.

I’m in the small blind with A♦9♥ and 23bb effective facing a button open to 3bb (pot 450, 250 to me). A9o is strong enough to defend vs a wide BTN opening and plays well postflop. A small 3-bet to the stated min (500) is too small to gain fold equity versus these players and will often get called, creating an awkward multiway OOP spot; shoving would be unnecessarily committal with 23bb. I’ll call the raise (total commitment 300) to see a flop and proceed postflop: look to c-bet or barreled value when I connect (A or hearts, good draws) and give up to heavy resistance on unfavorable, dry boards.

I am in the Big Blind holding Q5o. Elon (Button) raised to 300, and Minni (Small Blind) called. Although the pot odds are roughly 3.5:1 (200 to win 700), Q5o is a very weak hand that performs poorly post-flop, especially when out of position against two opponents. Minni is also extremely short-stacked, which increases the likelihood of an all-in situation that I would prefer to avoid with this hand. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Flop is Kc-4c-Ac. I hold Ad9h: top pair but a very vulnerable one — I have no club and the board is monotone. Villain opened from the button, so his range contains lots of Ax/Kx and a non-trivial number of club combos (roughly a few percent to have a made flush with two clubs in hand, and a larger chunk with a single club draw). With ~20.5bb effective and the pot already ~700, I want to control the pot and avoid bloating it with a marginal top pair that can be dominated or outdrawn. A small lead bet will be called by many draws/worse and a raise would be too committal; checking keeps the pot manageable, lets me see how he reacts, and preserves options to call a small c-bet or fold to heavy aggression. I’ll check and re-evaluate on his action (call modest bet, fold to a large shove/raise, or extract value on safe turn cards).

Flopped the nut flush (AcKc9c7c4c) on a monotone club board after raising pre-flop and getting called by SB Minni (short stack ~20BB). She checked, giving me initiative in position. This is a dream spot: bet for maximum value from her wide calling range including weaker flushes (worse kickers), top pair, sets, combo draws. Pot 700, her stack 2050; pot-sized bet of 700 puts strong pressure while allowing her to commit ~1/3 stack with worse hands. Builds pot for river potential, denies equity to draws. Optimal value bet in position with nuts vs calling station/short stack.

I have top pair (A♦9♥) on a monotone club flop. Elon’s button c-bet (700 into the pot) is reasonable but the chance he already holds a two-club flush is relatively small and many c-bets will be bluffs or worse Ax/Kx hands that I beat. Calling keeps the pot under control with a vulnerable but still strong hand, preserves fold equity on later streets, and gives correct pot odds (~3:1) to continue. Raising would bloat the pot with a hand that can be crushed by the rare flush and folding is too weak. Plan: call to see the turn, fold to a large raise/shove or to a club that completes obvious flushes, and extract value on safe turn cards.

Check. The turn 8c completes a club-heavy board (Kc-4c-Ac-8c) so any single club in Elon’s hand now makes a flush. I hold Ad9h — top pair but no club and a marginal kicker — and Elon opened wide from the button and c-bet the flop, so his range contains a lot of club combos and strong hands that beat me. Betting here only gets value from worse Aces and will often induce a raise from made flushes or commit me with a dominated hand; a blocking bet is too thin given stack depths. I’ll check to control the pot, keep the river decision flexible, and avoid bloating the pot when I’m frequently behind. Plan: if Elon leads the turn I’ll fold to heavy pressure / shove and only continue to reasonably small turn/river bets that give correct pot odds; if he checks back I’ll evaluate the river and proceed to showdown or call small bets.

Nut flush on a four-flush club board (AcKc8c4c), absolute nuts holding 9c7c. Heads-up vs short-stacked Minni (1350 left) who checked flop and turn after calling pre and flop bet. Pot 2100. She likely defends wide including weaker flushes (e.g., QcJc, flush draws completed), pairs with clubs, sets. Her all-in call range vs my bet includes plenty of worse hands for thin value. Bet sizing to her stack (1350) maximizes EV by denying her checking behind with showdown value or improving, builds biggest pot possible. No check as it risks giving free card or her folding worse. Standard jam for value vs short stack.

I have A♦9♥ on Kc–4c–Ac–8c. I checked the flop to control the pot and called the flop bet, but the turn 8c completes a four‑club board: any single club in Elon’s hand is now a flush. Elon opened from the button and barreled flop + turn — a line strongly weighted to flushes and strong value hands (clubs, A‑x/K‑x that play this way), not easy bluffs. The pot is 3450 and I must call 1350 (my entire stack); that requires ~28.6% equity to break even. Given the completed flush and the likelihood he holds at least one club or a better Ace, my actual showdown equity is well below that threshold. Committing my remaining stack with a vulnerable one‑pair, no club and a weak kicker is unnecessarily reckless. I’ll fold and preserve my stack for more favorable spots.

Stack = 1350 (≈13.5bb). Pot is 150 and it costs 100 to see the flop. My hand 4♦8♠ offsuit is weak, poorly connected and often dominated by the blinds’ defending ranges. Calling 100 gives me pot odds of 2.5:1 (break-even ≈28.6%), but my true equity multiway vs SB+BB is likely below that. Raising or shoving is a poor use of a short stack here — against two deep, aggressive players a shove would get called with many hands that crush me; a small open-raise will just buy a marginal pot and invite 3-bets. With ~13.5bb and a very weak holding, the correct play is to preserve chips and wait for a better shove/raise spot (pocket pair, broadway, or suited connector). Fold preflop.

I am in the Small Blind with 2h 6h. While the hand is suited, it is extremely weak. Since the Button has folded, it is just me and the Big Blind (Elon). Given the poor connectivity and low rank of my cards, and the fact that I would be out of position for the rest of the hand if I called, folding is the most prudent strategy to conserve my stack for higher-equity opportunities.

I am on the button with Qc4c, a decent suited semi-connector. Minni (the big blind) is extremely short-stacked with only 1,250 chips remaining. Given the stack sizes, if I raise, I should be prepared for Minni to potentially shove. However, a standard open raise is the correct play here to put pressure on both the small and big blinds. Opening for 300 (3x) is a standard play to take control of the hand in position.

T4s is a marginal suited connector, but from the small blind facing a button raise, it's a clear fold. Out of position postflop with poor playability, especially against Flash who has been aggressive historically. Pot odds are about 1.8:1 (250 to call into ~700), but equity realization OOP is low without implied odds against deep opponent. Minni is short-stacked but irrelevant as we shouldn't speculate here. Preserve stack for premium spots to maximize long-term EV over 150 hands.

I have Ad4d in the big blind facing a single-button open to 300. My stack is 1250 (~12.5bb) and the pot is 450 with 200 to call. With a short stack like this A4s is a shove-worthy holding: it gets maximum fold equity against the button’s open, avoids playing multi-street OOP with a marginal hand, and still has reasonable equity if called. A small 3-bet (to 500) or a flat call leaves me awkwardly shallow post‑flop and is lower EV than a clean push. I’ll shove to apply maximum pressure and either take down the pot immediately or go in with a hand that fares well vs a wide calling range.

I have already committed 300 chips into the pot. Minni, who started the hand with 1,350 chips, has moved all-in for 1,250 (a 3-bet). To call the remaining 950 chips, the pot is offering me 1,600:950 odds, which is approximately 1.68 to 1. My hand, Qc4c, has enough equity against a wide range of hands that Minni might shove with in this short-stack situation. Since I am getting good pot odds and Minni is now effectively all-in, I will call to see the flop.

I opened preflop to apply pressure and was called, leaving me with only 100 behind. On the flop (Js Jc 2d) I have A♦4♦: no pair, only Ace-high with a modest chance to improve (≈12.5% to hit an Ace by river, ≈4% backdoor diamond flush, ~16–17% combined vs a made Jack). The pot is huge (≈2550) compared to my remaining 100. Shoving/betting my 100 has almost no fold equity — an opponent only needs ≈3.8% equity to call 100 into this pot, so they will call with virtually any two cards. That means a bet here is rarely going to take the pot and is just an expensive plea to improve. Checking preserves my remaining chips and lets me realize equity for free if he checks, while still allowing me to call a small c-bet (<=100). If he barrels big I can fold and save chips for a better spot. I’ll check and reevaluate on the turn.

Minni is extremely short-stacked with only 100 chips remaining in a pot of 2,550. She 3-bet pre-flop and then checked this paired flop of Js Jc 2d. With Qc 4c, I have Queen high and a backdoor flush draw. Since the cost to put her all-in is negligible compared to the pot size (100 into 2,550), and there is no reason to let her see more cards for free if I can end the betting now, I will bet her remaining 100 chips. She is mathematically committed to calling with any two cards, so this effectively forces the showdown.

I checked the flop to preserve my short stack and realize equity. Flash’s tiny 100 bet into a ~2650 pot gives me absurd pot odds — break-even equity ≈ 100 / (2650+100) ≈ 3.6%. With Ad4d I have ~12.5% to pair an Ace by river and about ~4.2% to make a runner-runner diamond flush (plus other small improvement chances), so my chance to improve to a potentially winning hand is well above the break-even threshold. Flash called my shove preflop and is c-betting a wide range, so folding a single 100 here surrenders a lot of EV. Given the price, I must call (this is effectively an all-in for my remaining 100).

3-handed on the button facing an SB limp (pot 150, to call 100 into BB). T6o is a playable hand with straight potential and position advantage. SB (Minni) is relatively short at 27BB and limps suggest weakness or trapping, but button raises wide here to steal or isolate. Raising applies pressure preflop, and we have fold equity against both, especially short SB. Postflop, position allows us to realize equity well. Standard raise size around 3x BB.

I’m in the small blind with 4♣7♣ (suited) facing a button open to 300. My stack is 2,700 (~27bb) and it’s 250 more to call (300 total). 4♣7♣ is a speculative, playable hand that plays well postflop when I hit clubs or connected flops. Folding here is too tight given the pot odds (calling 250 into a 450 pot gives roughly 2.8:1), and a 3‑bet or isolation raise with this hand out of position is undesirable. I’ll call to see a flop, keep pot control, and proceed cautiously: continue with strong club/paired flops or good equity, fold to large aggression on dry misses, and avoid bloating the pot out of position without real equity.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Five offsuit. The Button (Elon) has raised to 3x the big blind, and the Small Blind (Minni) has called. Minni's stack is extremely short (only 2,450 chips total), which likely means she is playing a wide range of hands or looking for any spot to commit her remaining chips. My hand, A5o, is a solid hand to see a flop with in a three-way pot, especially getting excellent pot odds (calling 200 to win a pot that will be 900). Folding is out of the question given the price. Raising (3-betting) is an option, but with a very short-stacked player in the Small Blind who already called, a raise might commit me to an all-in situation against Minni where my A5o is only a marginal favorite or a slight underdog. Calling allows me to realize my equity and play post-flop, where I have position over Minni (though Elon has position over me).

I checked preflop with 4♣7♣ for pot odds; on this K♣ T♦ 5♠ flop I completely missed. I have no pair, no straight draw and only a runner‑runner club possibility (very low equity), and this is a 3‑way pot with me out of position against the original raiser and a cold caller. Bluffing multiway OOP is low EV and I don’t want to bloat the pot with junk given my ~24.5bb stack. I’ll check to control the pot and see a turn for free; plan is to fold to any meaningful bet (especially >~1/3–1/2 pot) and only continue if I pick up a club or a pair or get very cheap pot odds to peel a small c‑bet.

On a board of Td-5s-Kc, I have middle pair with an Ace kicker. While I have a piece of the board, King and Ten are very much in the range of the pre-flop aggressor (Elon). Minni has checked, and since I am out of position against Elon, checking is the most prudent move. This allows me to see how the original raiser reacts to this texture and keep the pot manageable while I have a medium-strength hand.

Flop of Td 5s Kc gives me top pair tens (Th) with a weak 6 kicker, but crucially an open-ended straight draw (5-6-T, 8 outs to the nuts). Combined, excellent equity (~40-50% vs typical calling ranges). Preflop I raised as button, they flat-called, suggesting a wide range without premium hands. Both checked to me, indicating possible weakness, especially Minni who is short-stacked at 24BB. Betting builds the pot for value, protects against draws (67, A4 for wheel? but less likely), and applies max pressure on Minni who might fold or shove light. Flash deep but checked BB suggests not super strong. Half-pot bet (450) is standard, but sizing to 600 (2/3 pot) balances value/protection/FE well, denying equity to floats/draws. Position allows control postflop.

I have 4♣7♣ on K♣ T♦ 5♠ in a three‑way pot, out of position. I have no pair and only a backdoor club (needs both turn+river) plus a small chance to pair a hole card — realistic equity to the river is well under the ~29% break‑even needed to call 600 into the 1500 pot (600/(1500+600) ≈ 28.6%). Elon was the preflop raiser and sized a sizeable c‑bet (600 into 900) after both players checked, which looks like a standard continuation bet and is hard to bluff multiple players out of. Raising as a bluff here is low EV multiway and would commit too much of my ~24.5bb stack. Folding preserves chips and waits for a better spot.

I have bottom pair (Fives) with an Ace kicker on a board of King-Ten-Five. While the King and Ten are heavily in Elon's pre-flop raising range, his 600-chip bet into a 900-chip pot is a standard continuation bet that he might make with a wide variety of hands, including straight draws like QJ or total air. Given that we are now heads-up and I have a piece of the board with a high kicker, I have enough equity to call one street to see a turn card. If he continues his aggression on the turn, I will likely evaluate a fold unless I improve.

The turn card is the King of Hearts, which pairs the top card of the flop. I currently hold two pair (Kings and Fives) with an Ace kicker. However, since I am out of position against the pre-flop aggressor, checking is the most appropriate move. The second King on the board reduces the likelihood that Elon has a King, but if he does, he now has trips. By checking, I can see how Elon reacts to the board pairing. If he checks back, I likely have the best hand or he is giving up on a draw. If he bets, I will have to evaluate the size and his likely range, but for now, checking maintains pot control with a marginal hand.

Flash check-called a sizable flop bet on KcTd5s, indicating strength such as Kx, Tx, mid-pairs, or draws. The turn Kh pairs the board, improving any Kx to two pair and making the board more dangerous for my top pair tens with a weak 6 kicker. Flash checks again, which could be a trap with improved hands (two pair, sets, KK) or weakness (missed draw, weaker pair), but his range hits kings often enough that continuing aggression risks being behind and facing raises. Pot is small relative to stacks (2100 vs ~14k effective), so checking for pot control is optimal: see river free in position, showdown if checks through, or fold to river bet without further loss. Preflop raise and flop bet already applied pressure, extracting value from worse; no need to overplay vulnerable one-pair hand on paired board. Prioritizing stack preservation in marathon to 150 hands.

On the river, the board is Td-5s-Kc-Kh-2s. I have a pair of Fives, which combined with the pair of Kings on the board, gives me two pair (KK55A). Elon, the pre-flop aggressor, bet the flop but checked back on the turn when the second King appeared. This check-back suggests he likely doesn't have a King and might be holding a Ten, a smaller pocket pair, or a missed draw like QJ. My pair of Fives has some showdown value, but it is not strong enough to bet for value, as most hands that call me will likely have me beaten (like a Ten or a better pocket pair). By checking, I can potentially catch a bluff from his missed draws or get to a cheap showdown against his weaker range. Betting risks getting raised or called only by better hands.

River brings 2s, completing some straight draws (e.g., 34) or backdoor spades. Flash's check-call on flop (K-high) followed by checking turn (paired K) and now river suggests possible king (two pair or trips), mid-pair, or missed draw. My Th6d is pair of tens, decent but crushed by any Kx which he reps strongly. Preflop raise + flop bet reps strength (KK/TK/sets), so he checks weaker hands. Pot control remains priority: checking wins if ahead (worse pairs/draws) without bloating pot or facing raise. Deep stacks (14k+ effective), 36 hands left; no need to bluff or thin-value thin air. Free showdown maximizes EV long-term.

I’m on the button with K♣9♥ and ~24.5BB (stack 2450). The pot is 150 and it’s folded to me — to call 100 or raise (min to 200). In a 3-handed spot K9o is a reasonable open. I’ll raise to 300 (3x the big blind) instead of limping or min-raising: this sizing matches the table standard, gives meaningful fold equity, and reduces the likelihood of cheap multiway pots while leaving plenty of chips to play postflop. I’m not shoving — opponents are deep and will call shoves with wide ranges, creating unnecessary variance. Plan: if called, use position to c-bet favorable flops (and play pot-control on scary boards); fold to large 3-bets/shoves where K9o is likely crushed. This play maximizes my expected value here while preserving the stack for the remaining hands.

I am in the Small Blind with 3s 6s. Minni, who is playing on a very short stack (around 21BBs), has opened to 300 from the button. While 3s 6s is suited, it is an extremely weak hand to play out of position, especially against a short-stacker where my implied odds are limited. Calling would put me in a difficult spot post-flop, and folding is the most disciplined play to maintain my chip lead in the game.

Preflop in the big blind facing a button raise to 300 from Minni, who plays aggressively and raises frequently from late position. My hand is 4c5d, offsuit low connectors with minimal playability, poor post-flop potential OOP, especially against a wide button range. Pot odds are 200 to call into 450 (about 3.25:1), but implied odds are low given Minni's short stack of 2150 and my deep stack. Standard fold to preserve stack and wait for better spots. No history this hand indicates strength.

I am on the button with 7s 4d, which is a very weak hand. Even in a three-handed game, 74o is generally not worth playing. Minni, in the big blind, is short-stacked with only 2500 chips, which makes her more likely to shove over a raise. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Preflop, folded to me in SB with 6d Js offsuit. Pot is 150 (SB 50 + BB 100), need to call 50 more for 3:1 odds. J6o has reasonable equity against BB's wide range (~45-50% vs limping range), and with good pot odds, completing is +EV. OOP postflop, but Minni is short-stacked at 25BB (2500), so if I hit, can stack her; if she shoves, easy fold. Raising not justified with this hand. Folding too tight given the price. Will play cautiously postflop, check-fold weak boards.

I have pocket eights (8c 8d) in the big blind facing a limp from the small blind (Elon). Heads-up this is a strong, playable hand that I want to isolate — raising protects my pair, denies cheap multi-way flops and gets value/fold equity. I’ll raise to 400 total (≈3–4x BB) which is a standard isolation size: it cuts out many speculative hands, still leaves me ~2,100 behind for postflop play, and gives good fold equity. Plan: if called I’ll play cautiously OOP (c-bet favorable textures, pot-control on A/K-high boards, set-mine when appropriate); if Elon 3-bets small I’ll consider calling for set value, but I’ll fold or strongly re-evaluate to very large 3-bets/4-bets or shoves given stack-to-pot dynamics and his range.

Preflop in SB with J6o offsuit after limping. Minni raises to 400 total from BB (short at 22BB). Pot 500, to call 300 (getting ~2.67:1). J6o has marginal equity (~30-35% vs wide BB raise range after limp), but terrible playability OOP, dominated often, reverse implied odds vs shortstack. Postflop, Minni likely to shove wide on most boards, hard to realize equity OOP. Deep stacked at 168BB, no need to gamble in tough spot. Folding preserves stack for better positions/hands. 3bet bluff risky vs shortstack who shoves light. Clear fold.

3-handed on the button with 5d6s suited connectors, which is a strong speculative hand in this spot due to position, implied odds, and wide button opening ranges. Pot is 150 with blinds posted (SB 50, BB 100), no prior action. Minni is short-stacked at ~26BB in SB, likely to fold or shove; Flash deep in BB. Standard play is to raise to 3x BB (300 total) to steal blinds or build pot with position. Folding is too tight; limping too passive multiway.

Small blind with T8d in a 3-handed pot: this is a defendable, suited one-gapper with decent playability. Pot is 450 and I need to put in 250 more (300 total). Calling keeps the pot under control, realizes implied odds vs a wide button open, and preserves my 2650 stack for postflop play. A min-3bet to 500 would be too light with this holding (and invites callers/4‑bets), while folding is too tight given the hand's equity and deep stacks. Plan: call to see flop; continue with top pair, good draws, or two-pair+; exercise pot control or fold to heavy aggression on dry misses.

I am in the Big Blind with 3h 4s. Elon opened from the button for 300 and Minni called from the Small Blind. I need to call 200 more into a pot of 700. While the pot odds are roughly 4.5:1, 43o is one of the weakest possible starting hands and has extremely poor playability post-flop, especially out of position in a multi-way pot. Minni's short stack also makes the hand more dangerous to play if she commits on the flop. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Flop is 3s-6h-5s — very dry and uncoordinated. I hold Td8d: two overcards and backdoor diamond/runner-runner straight potential, but no pair and very little immediate equity. Elon was the preflop raiser, so his c-bet frequency is high, but I don’t want to bloat the pot with a weak hand out of position. Plan is to control the pot and realize equity: check and evaluate his reaction. If he checks back I get a free turn; if he c-bets small (roughly ≤ half pot, ~350) I will call to see a turn and fold to heavy turn/river aggression unless I improve (pair, diamond, or strong straight-card). If he fires a larger bet I’ll fold and preserve stack. Action: check to keep options and avoid committing with air.

Flop is 3s 6h 5s, giving me top two pair (6s/6h and 5d/5s) with a spade blocker on a highly drawy board featuring a spade flush draw and multiple straight draws (2-4 or 4-7). Minni checked from SB after calling my button raise pre-flop, indicating possible weakness or trapping, but her range includes many draws, pairs, and floats. Pot is 700 with her effective stack of 2400 (~24BB), so betting for value while protecting against draws and denying equity. Half-pot bet of 400 balances value from worse pairs/top pair/draws and induces shoves from weaker hands she might commit with. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or allowing bluffs, suboptimal with such a strong hand in position heads-up.

Board is very dry (3s-6h-5s). I hold Td8d: two overcards and backdoor diamonds (11 diamonds left → ~5% chance to hit a runner-runner flush) but no made hand or immediate straight draw. Elon was the preflop raiser and c-bet a small size (400 into a 1100 pot ≈36% pot); calling 400 to win 1500 gives ~26.7% pot odds. Versus a wide c-bet range in a 3-handed game my combination of overcards + backdoors likely has roughly that equity, so calling to realize equity and keep the pot manageable OOP is correct. Folding is too tight and raising is reckless with air out of position. Plan: call to see the turn; fold to significant turn aggression if I miss, but if I pick up a diamond or pair (T/8) I will proceed aggressively to protect/extract value.

Turn brought the Qc and I remain unimproved with Td8d. Elon (preflop raiser) c-bet the flop and then checked the turn — that line often indicates weakness or pot control rather than a polarization that I can exploit OOP. I have 17 clear outs to improve by the river (11 diamonds + 3 remaining Tens + 3 remaining Eights) ≈ 17/46 ≈ 37% to make at least a pair or a flush. Pot is 1500 and there is nothing to call now. Betting or raising out of position with pure air here would bloat the pot and risk a big portion of my 2000 stack with limited fold equity; a small blocker bet could be raised and put me to a painful decision. Best line is pot control and to realize equity: check and see the river. Plan for the river: if I pair or make a diamond I can bet/extract; if I miss I will fold to significant aggression. Action: check to keep the pot manageable and preserve fold equity/stack.

On the turn Qc board (3s6h5sQc), I hold top two pair (6s/6h, 5d/5s) with a spade blocker, a very strong hand heads-up in position against Minni's short stack of 2000 (1.33x pot). The board remains draw-heavy (spade flush draw, incomplete straight draws like 24, 47), and the Qc bricks most but keeps some equity live for her possible draws or Ax floats. Minni checked flop OOP after calling my raise pre, now checks turn again, representing weakness, overpairs, TPGK, weaker pairs, or draws in her calling range. Checking risks free cards to draws completing or allowing bluffs/check-raises. Betting extracts value from her worse made hands (pairs, two-pair worse like 65/35), induces calls/shoves from draws she might commit with short, and denies equity. Sizing 1200 (~80% pot) pressures her effective stack well: she calls leaving 800 (can hero-call weaker), shoves over puts me in good spot, folds lose less. Larger pot-bet risks fold equity too high from marginal value hands; smaller under-reps strength. Consistent with flop c-bet line, builds big pot when ahead in this 3-handed dynamic to maximize EV over 150 hands.

I fold. On the Qc turn I have T♦8♦ with no pair and effectively only 6 clean river outs (three Tens + three Eights) — about 6/46 ≈ 13% to improve to a pair. The pot is 2,700 and Elon’s raise requires 1,200 to call, so I need ≈1200/(2700+1200)=30.8% equity to make a call profitable. My equity (~13%) is far below that. Elon was the preflop raiser and fired the flop small then barreled the turn large — a line consistent with having picked up a Queen, an overpair/set, or a legitimate value range; while he may bluff sometimes, my hand has too little showdown value and poor fold equity. Shoving all‑in as a bluff is also unattractive — he has a deep stack and will call many hands that dominate me. Earlier I planned to check and realize equity; faced with this significant turn aggression, folding preserves my 2,000 stack for better, +EV spots.

On the button with AdQd (AQ suited) — a top-tier late-position hand. Pot is 150 and it’s 100 to call; my stack is 2000 (≈20bb). Standard +EV play is to open-raise to ~3x the BB to take the initiative, apply pressure to the blinds, and deny good odds to speculative hands. Raising to 300 total (3x) maximizes fold equity and isolates opponents while keeping postflop play manageable with position; if I face a large 3-bet I can fold and preserve my stack, but if I get called I have strong equity to play from the button.

I am in the Small Blind with KJo, a strong starting hand in a 3-handed game. Minni, who is on the Button, has raised to 300. Minni's stack is quite short at 1700 chips (17 big blinds). KJo dominates much of a standard button opening range. While I could 3-bet to isolate, Minni's short stack means that any significant raise will likely result in an all-in confrontation. By calling, I keep the pot manageable and can evaluate the flop, while also potentially keeping Elon in the hand with worse cards. Given the pot odds (calling 250 to win a pot of 450), calling is a very standard and profitable play here.

In the big blind facing a button min-raise to 300 and small blind flat call, creating a multiway pot. My J9o is offsuit with marginal playability out of position. Postflop, it plays poorly multiway against likely calling ranges including suited connectors, broadways, and pairs from both opponents. Pot odds are about 3.5:1 (calling 200 to win 700), but implied odds are low given stacks and multiway dynamic. Opponents have been aggressive historically (Minni raises frequently), but folding preserves stack for better spots in this long cash game session. No strong reason to 3-bet or call.

The flop is 3-4-3, a paired and relatively dry board. I have King-high with overcards to the small cards on the board. Since I am out of position against the pre-flop aggressor (Minni), the standard play is to check and see how she proceeds. Minni has a very short stack (1400 remaining), so any bet she makes will be significant. By checking, I can evaluate her action and decide if my King-high is worth continuing with based on the price she offers.

I raised preflop with A♦Q♦ and was called by the SB. Flop 3♠ 4♥ 3♥ is very dry and paired low — there are few realistic draws and many of the SB’s preflop calling hands (suited gappers, broadways, small pairs) miss this board. I have two clean overcards and an Ace-blocker to many A-high combos, and I’m in position. A continuation bet here leverages my initiative and fold equity to take down the pot or get value from worse hands that call. With ~17BB effective and a 700-chip pot, a ~1/2-pot c-bet (350) applies meaningful pressure without committing my stack unnecessarily. If he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn (bet for value if I pair or improve, pot-control or fold to heavy aggression if I miss). If he raises big I can fold against a strong line since my equity vs sets/trips is poor. Bet half-pot to maximize immediate EV and keep postflop choices manageable.

On this paired flop of 3s-4h-3h, my King-high (KdJs) still has significant showdown value and two overcards against Minni's wide button opening range. Minni's half-pot continuation bet is standard. Given the pot odds (calling 350 to win a total pot of 1050) and the likelihood that I am still ahead or have sufficient equity to continue, calling is the most appropriate action. Minni is short-stacked (1350 remaining), so I need to be careful, but folding K-high on this specific board texture is too exploitable.

On the turn, an Ace (As) has appeared, which is a scare card. I am out of position against Minni, who is the pre-flop aggressor and has a very short stack remaining (1350 chips). I currently only have King-high. Checking is the most prudent action here to see how Minni responds to the Ace. If she checks behind, I may still have the best hand or can evaluate on the river. If she shoves, I will have to decide if my King-high has enough equity against her specific range, but for now, checking is the standard play.

I opened pre with A♦Q♦, c‑bet the very dry paired flop and was called. The A♠ on the turn gives me top pair/top kicker. Flash called the flop and now checks — a line that often contains medium pocket pairs (44, 55, 66), floats (KQ/KJ, suited connectors) or worse A‑x; strong full house/quads lines are unlikely because he didn’t raise earlier. Pot = 1,400 and my effective stack = 1,350 (SPR ≈ 0.96), so this is a low‑SPR value spot where I should extract now. I’ll bet about 2/3 of the pot (~900) to charge worse pairs and worse A’s, deny a free river, and simplify decisions. If Flash check‑raises large (polarizing to full house/quads) I’ll fold; small raises I’ll evaluate but this sizing maximizes value from the hands that will call.

Minni, who is playing a relatively short stack, has committed a significant portion of her remaining chips (900 out of 1350) on the turn. The Ace on the turn is a card that very strongly hits her pre-flop button-raising range. With only King-high and no draw, I am likely behind her value range (any Ace, any pocket pair, or a 3) and even trailing some of her potential semi-bluffs (like heart draws). Given she has only 450 chips left behind, this bet is essentially committing her to the pot. Calling 900 with no pair and no draw is a losing play in the long run. I will fold and look for a better opportunity.

I am on the button with 8d 2h. This is a very weak hand. While I'm in position, Minni is in the Big Blind with a very short stack (2650), which makes them more likely to shove or defend aggressively. There's no reason to invest chips with such a poor starting hand in this spot. I will fold.

Td5h is a complete trash hand: offsuit, poorly connected, low cards with minimal playability or equity against any reasonable defending range from Minni in the big blind. Even though the pot lays good odds to complete the small blind (pot will be 200 to win 200 for 50 more), the hand performs terribly post-flop out of position, especially against a short stack like Minni who might defend wide or shove. No steal value here as it's too weak to raise profitably. Folding preserves my deep stack (174 BB effective) for premium spots in the late stages of the game.

Preflop on the button with Jd9s, 3-handed. Pot of 150 with to-call 100 indicates SB (Minni) folded after posting their blind, leaving dead money and facing only BB (Flash). Position is excellent, J9o is well within a button opening range vs BB, suitable for value, bluffs, and stealing the blinds + dead SB money. Minni is short-stacked at 2750 but out. Both I and Flash are deep-stacked, but stealing works here. Raising to 3BB (300 total) is standard sizing to build pot in position while maintaining fold equity. Folding or limping is suboptimal.

I’m in the small blind with Q♦4♦ facing a button open to 300 (pot 450). Q4s is a marginal but suited hand — in a 3-handed game the button’s opening range is quite wide so this hand has some equity, but it plays poorly out of position postflop. A light 3-bet here is unattractive (we’d be out of position and our hand is not strong enough to take the initiative reliably), and folding is too tight given the price. I’ll call the 250 (total commit 300) to see a flop, plan to fold to a sizable 3-bet, and proceed cautiously on the flop: continue only with a real piece (Q, pair, good diamond draw) or use pot control and check-fold against heavy action.

I am in the Big Blind holding J8o. The Button (Elon) has raised to 3x, and the Small Blind (Minni) has called. Minni is quite short-stacked with only 2500 total. I need to call 200 more to see a flop in a pot that is already 700. The pot odds are 200 to win a total pot of 900, which is 22.2%. While J8o is not a premium hand, it is strong enough to defend the Big Blind against a button open when getting these odds, especially closing the action. I will call and see a flop.

Flop: 5s 5c Ah. I have Q♦4♦ — no pair, no diamonds on board (so a flush is impossible), only decent outs are pairing the Q or the 4 (very low equity). This is a 3‑way pot with healthy stacks (Elon BTN 17,175, Flash BB 19,425) and the preflop opener’s range contains lots of A‑x and many hands that already beat me (pocket 5s, Ax, overpairs). Betting out OOP with such a weak hand is a loser’s line: I don’t want to build the pot or turn my marginal holding into a bluff against two players. I’ll check and keep the pot small. Plan: fold to any meaningful bet — our hand has poor showdown equity — but be open to calling a very tiny probe (a small sizing I can exploit as a float vs a known aggressive player); otherwise give up and re‑evaluate if the turn pairs me or brings a meaningful change in texture.

On a flop of 5s-5c-Ah, the board is very static and favors the pre-flop aggressor's range (Elon), which contains many Aces. Holding Jack-high with no draw, I have very little equity in this pot. Since Minni checked, I will also check to see if we can get a free turn card or see how Elon reacts. I have no reason to bet into two opponents on this board.

Flop is 5s5c Ah, a paired board with top pair possible, but both opponents checked, suggesting weakness or traps. As preflop raiser on the button with position, this is a standard spot for a continuation bet with Jd9s (complete air, jack-high, backdoor spade draw). 3-handed play favors aggression; c-bet takes down the pot frequently against checks. Pot is 900, betting half-pot (450) balances range, applies pressure on Minni's short stack (2500), and builds pot OOP. Checking back risks giving free cards to draws or allowing them to realize equity cheaply. Bet sizing 450 maintains fold equity while not overbetting.

Fold. On 5♠5♣A♥ with Q♦4♦ I have no pair and no relevant backdoor; my only clean outs are three queens and three fours (≈6 outs → ~24% to hit by the river). Calling 450 into a 1,350 pot (would make the pot 1,800) requires ≈25% equity to breakeven, and that’s before accounting for the fact this is a 3‑way pot and that a made queen often still loses to Ax or better hands Elon (button) is likely continuing with. Elon has been an aggressive opener/CBettor, so this sizing looks like standard value or a polar C‑bet I can’t credibly contest out of position. A raise/bluff here is too risky with poor blockers and only 25BB effective stacks. Preserve chips and fold.

On a flop of 5s-5c-Ah, the board heavily favors the pre-flop aggressor (Elon) who likely has many Aces in his range. With only Jack high and no draw, I have almost zero equity against a bet. Minni has already folded, and calling here would be a waste of chips given the lack of potential for my hand to improve to the winner. I will fold.

2♣9♦ is one of the weakest hands possible. Although I’m on the button in a three‑handed pot, my stack is only 25 BB and both opponents have shown frequent aggression/3‑betting in previous hands. Raising to try to steal risks a cold 3‑bet or tough postflop decisions with virtually no equity; calling 100 to see a flop is also low EV since the hand rarely improves and will be dominated. Preserve the stack and wait for a playable spot. Fold.

With the button (Minni) folding, the action is on me in the Small Blind. I have Ace-Ten offsuit (As Th), which is a very strong starting hand in a heads-up scenario against the Big Blind. I will put in a standard open-raise to 300 to put pressure on Elon and potentially take down the blinds immediately or play a strong hand with position post-flop if called.

Facing a standard 3x open raise from Flash in the small blind, a position with a wide stealing range. My 67o is a speculative hand with straight potential, decent playability, and good implied odds deep-stacked (my stack ~18k, pot 400, calling 200 more for total 300 committed). Pot odds are favorable (~2:1 direct, but realizing equity heads-up vs wide range). Folding is too tight in BB defense; 3-betting as a bluff is possible but unnecessary with this hand's postflop potential. Calling to see a cheap flop and play in position postflop.

I have top pair with an Ace and a Ten kicker on a board that contains both a flush draw and potential straight draws. I am out of position against Elon, who called from the Big Blind. To extract value from weaker Aces and to charge the various draws, I will lead with a continuation bet of 400, which is approximately two-thirds of the pot.

Preflop: Defended wide in BB vs SB open with playable 67o suited? (6h7d offsuit actually, but good connectors). Flop gives bottom pair (6s) + gutshot straight draw (8 outs to 9 or 5 for straight, ~16% equity standalone) + backdoor flush potential. Total equity ~25-35% vs Flash's polarized SB c-bet range (Ax value, heart draws, gutshots, air bluffs). Pot odds 1400:400 (~3.5:1, need 22%) excellent. Deep stacks (17k+ eff) provide implied odds to crack Ax or hit straight/set. Folding too tight on defended hand; raising overplays vulnerable bottom pair on draw-heavy Ah8h6c board where he'll fold worse but call/fold to better. Call to pot control, realize equity OOP, re-eval turn. History shows Flash aggressive c-bettor, often bluffing flops.

I have trip Aces with a Ten kicker. The second Ace on the turn significantly improves my hand. Given that Elon called the flop, he likely has an 8, a 6, a flush draw, or a weaker Ace. I will bet 900 into the 1400 pot to extract value from his calling range and charge any potential draws.

Deep effective stacks (~17k) vs aggressive SB stealer Flash who frequently c-bets flops and continues with polarized range. Preflop defended wide BB with suited connectors. Flop 6-Ah-8h: bottom pair + double gutshot (8 outs to straight: 4x5,4x9 ~17% river), backdoor potential. Called small c-bet 400 into ~1400 for good odds. Turn Ac pairs top, scary for draws/bluffs but Flash bombs 900 (~2/3 pot into 2300 post-call). Range: value Ax (TPTK now two pair, AA trips, sets); bluffs (missed Khx hearts, gutshots like 57/79/49, air). Blocks some 66. Equity est. 25-35% vs polarized (beats bluffs/draws, some fold equity implied river; loses to Ax but not always stacked). Pot odds 900/3200=22% direct + massive implied (eff stacks >> pot). OOP call pot-controls, realizes equity without overplaying vulnerable one-pair/gutshot. Raise folds out bluffs we beat, gets called by better; fold too tight vs aggro history (e.g., Hand 108 raised river bluff folded to Flash reraise). Call to river, re-eval (check or value if improves, hero-call bluffs).

I have the absolute nuts. I hold the Ace of Spades, and with three Aces on the board (Heart, Club, and Diamond), I have Quad Aces. Given the board texture, there is no possible straight flush. Elon has called bets on every street, suggesting he has a strong hand, likely a Full House (Aces full of eights, sixes, or a pocket pair). I will make a large value bet of 2500 chips, which is roughly 78% of the pot, expecting him to call with his entire range of Full Houses.

Board has three aces (Ah, Ac, Ad), 8h, 6c, giving everyone AAA86 baseline. My 6h7d makes AAA87 (aces with 8-7 kickers), beating unpaired hands with lower kickers (e.g., AAA86, AAA85, QJ etc.) and some bluffs/missed draws. Beats all pure air/bluffs. Loses to pocket pairs (aces full of xx), 66/88 (full houses), higher unpaired kickers like AAAKQ/KJ/T9+, Asx (quads). Flash's SB 3-bet pot OOP vs wide BB defend range includes Ax suited (but only As for quads), suited connectors (e.g., 57 straight flop, 97/49 gutshots, heart draws like KhQh/JTh missed), bluffs. Aggro history (e.g., Hand 108 river reraise bluff, Hand 77 big river bet), small flop bet suggests not monster then, continued barreling on paired boards likely polarized: nuts/full houses/high kickers (40-50%) vs bluffs/missed draws (50-60%). Equity ~40-50% (beats 100% bluffs, ~10% value like low pairs/miss; pot odds 5700:2500=2.3:1 need 30%). Deep eff stacks (~15k), prior calls committed, hero-call realizes equity vs aggro range. Raise risks folding out bluffs we crush, called by better; fold too tight OOP after pot building. Call maximizes EV.

I am on the button with Ten-Seven offsuit. While I have a dominant chip lead, Ten-Seven offsuit is a marginal hand to open with, especially with a short stack in the Big Blind (Minni has only 24 BB) who might be looking for a spot to shove. I'll play it safe and wait for a better hand to utilize my chip lead.

Preflop in SB vs BB, Flash folded. Pot 150, need to call 50 more with Jd4c offsuit, a weak hand but suitable for SB steal range as we have position postflop effectively in heads-up and BB defends wide but short-stacked at 2400. Raising small to 300 total (3x BB) applies pressure, has fold equity, and if called we have decent blocker with J and can play postflop. Folding loses blinds unnecessarily, limping OOP with weak hand is passive and invites trouble. Minni aggressive but short stack may fold or shove, both fine.

I have A♠Q♥ — a premium hand to 3-bet vs a single raiser. Elon opened to 300; calling 200 more keeps him cheap and lets him realize equity OOP. I want value and fold equity while keeping the pot manageable with my 2400 stack, so I will 3-bet to ~3x the open. Raising to 900 (total commit) puts pressure on his wide opening range, extracts value from worse aces and broadways, and leaves me ~1500 behind to play postflop. If he 4-bets large I’ll re-evaluate (likely fold to an over-shove, call smaller 4-bets depending on size and read).

J4o is a trash hand that I raised as a blind steal from SB, but Minni's aggressive 3-bet to 900 shows strength, likely a premium or strong hand given her history of aggression but this sizing OOP vs my raise. Pot 1200, calling 600 OOP (pot odds ~2:1, need ~33% equity) with poor playability, dominated vs her range (pairs, broadways, Ax), low equity (~25% max). Raising or shoving into short stack (1600) without fold equity or value disastrous. Folding loses only 300, preserves stack for better spots. Standard fold to 3-bet with this hand.

Preflop on the button with Qc6d, offsuit and poorly connected. Even in a short-handed game with 3 players, this is a weak hand without much playability or showdown value. Pot is 150 with to-call 100, offering about 2.5:1 odds, but position alone doesn't justify calling with such a marginal holding against potentially strong ranges from blinds who have shown aggression in history. Minni has a short stack of 2750, but folding preserves stack for better spots. Standard fold to avoid spew.

I’m in the small blind with 9♦5♣ (off‑suit). Pot is 150 and I must call 50 to see the flop; the big blind (Flash) is deep-stacked and has shown willingness to defend and play aggressively postflop. 9‑5o has very poor playability out of position and is frequently dominated by the BB’s calling range. Raising to 200 could win the pot immediately if he folds, but given his tendencies and stack size he is likely to call and force me into difficult OOP decisions with a weak hand. Calling 50 is cheap but still leaves me OOP with minimal equity or fold equity. With 27.5 BBs and many hands left, the lowest-variance, highest long-term EV line is to fold and wait for a better spot rather than invest more with this marginal hand.

I’m on the button with Q♠7♦ (off-suit) and ~27.5bb effective. Pot is 150 and it’s 100 to call — Q7o is a marginal hand to play multiway, but on the button I can take the initiative and try to steal the blinds. The table’s common open size is ~300, so I’ll raise to 300 to apply pressure and keep the pot a reasonable size. Plan: if both opponents fold I pick up the blinds; if I get called I’ll play postflop in position (c-bet on dry, favorable flops, and control/let go when I face strong resistance). I will fold to a large 3-bet — this hand is not worth risking a big portion of my stack out of position.

I am holding 9h 3s in the small blind. Minni, who has a relatively short stack of 24.5 BBs, has raised to 3 BBs from the button. My hand is extremely weak and plays poorly out of position. Given the stack sizes and the strength of my hand, there is no profitable way to continue. I will fold.

Minni raised to 300 from the button after SB folded, indicating a wide stealing range given position and short stack of 2450 (~24BB). My Ad6d is a strong suited ace hand with excellent blockers to AA/AK/AQ/AT, crushing much of Minni's calling range and holding good equity vs a shoving range (~45%+ vs typical shortstack jam range). OOP but heads-up now. Pot odds good but 3betting applies fold equity, builds pot OOP with strong hand, or isolates if called/shoved. Raising to 900 (3x) is balanced size, denies equity, and maximizes EV long-term.

I opened to 300 on the button with Q♠7♦ and Elon 3‑bet to 900. Pot is 1,250 and it’s 600 to call; calling would commit another 600 and leave me ~1,550 (≈15.5bb) behind. Q7o is a marginal, often‑dominated hand vs a standard 3‑bet range; Elon has shown frequent aggression and isn’t likely to fold to a thin 4‑bet. Calling puts me in awkward postflop spots with poor equity, and a 4‑bet shove is too thin and unnecessarily high variance with this holding. I’ll preserve my stack for better spots and fold here.

3d 4c is a very weak starting hand. Even though I am on the button, this hand lacks the high-card strength or connectivity to play profitably, particularly with Minni being short-stacked and likely looking for opportunities to shove or defend her blind aggressively. Folding is the most disciplined play.

Preflop in the small blind with 8s3c offsuit, a complete trash hand with no high card value, straight potential, or suitedness. Flash has folded on the button, leaving heads-up vs Minni in BB who has 2350 chips (~23BB). Completing the SB for 50 into 150 pot offers pot odds but this hand has terrible equity against any reasonable BB defending range and poor playability postflop. Standard fold to avoid difficult spots OOP with no showdown value or bluff equity. Preserve stack for better spots.

Preflop on the button with no action ahead (pot 150 suggests standard blinds posted, folds to me). QdTs is a solid opening hand from the button due to position advantage, decent playability, straight potential, and queen high. Raising isolates the short-stacked SB (Minni 2450) who may fold or commit, while BB (Flash deep) defends wide but I have position. Standard button open size of 3x BB (300) builds pot with initiative. Calling limps OOP multiway is poor; folding too tight for position.

AQs in the small blind facing a button open to 300 in a 3-handed pot is a clear 3-bet candidate. Calling keeps me out of position and likely multiway (big blind can defend), while a 3-bet both isolates the button and builds fold equity against his wide opening range. I want a size that applies pressure but leaves room to play postflop with my ~24.5BB stack, so I raise to 1,000 total (≈3.3x the open). That size is big enough to discourage light calls from the big blind, gives me initiative if called, and still leaves me ~1,500 behind to navigate postflop. If I face a shove/very large 4-bet I will re-evaluate (AQs is often a fold vs a tight shove), but versus calls/smaller 4-bets this play maximizes my expected value.

I am holding a premium hand, Ad Jd, in the Big Blind. Elon opened from the Button, and Minni (who is extremely short-stacked with only 1,500 total) raised to 1,000, leaving herself with just 500 chips. This is an ideal situation to 4-bet to isolate the short-stack. By raising to 2,500, I effectively put Minni all-in and put Elon in a difficult spot where he must decide whether to continue for a significantly larger price. Ad Jd performs very well against the wide ranges both opponents are likely to have here, and isolating helps simplify the hand while maximizing value.

Facing a 3-bet from SB Minni (short stack with 1500 behind) and a 4-bet to 2500 from deep BB Flash after my button open with QTo offsuit. QTo is offsuit, dominated by typical 3bet/4bet ranges (AQ+, TT+, AK etc.), poor equity multiway or heads-up (~30-35% vs tight range, worse vs polarized). Pot odds 2200 into 6000 (~27%) insufficient given reverse implied odds vs Minni's short stack and Flash's depth for value. Folding preserves stack (13k+), avoids spew in tough spot OOP postflop without position advantage continuing. Standard fold.

I 3‑bet to 1,000 to isolate the opener; Flash has just 3‑bet to 2,500 and Elon folded, so it’s heads‑up. Calling requires 1,500 more (my remaining stack) and is effectively an all‑in. The pot is ~3,800 so calling 1,500 more gives me ~5,300 to win — I only need ≈28% equity to break even. A♣Q♣ has well over that vs a reasonable BB 3‑bet range (pairs, AK, broadways), and Flash has shown an aggressive, not ultra‑tight profile, so I expect enough non‑value hands/bluffs in his range. With my short stack the pot odds and equity make calling the +EV play; folding would surrender my investment and give up good equity. I will call (get it in).

I am on the button with 7c4c, a suited connector that is playable but weak. The stack depths are relatively deep (I have over 260bb, Elon has about 135bb). In a heads-up or small-blind vs big-blind situation, 74s can be used as a speculative raise or a call. However, given the structure of this game and previous tendencies where players 3-bet frequently, I will fold this weak holding to avoid playing a potentially bloated pot out of position post-flop, especially against Elon who has shown aggression in previous hands.

Heads-up pre-flop from the button (SB) with QJo offsuit, a strong hand in this spot. Standard to raise for initiative and to deny equity to weaker hands in BB. Raising to 3x BB (300 total) is balanced and applies pressure. Position post-flop allows us to play profitably.

I hold Ace-Queen offsuit in the Big Blind. Elon, the button/small blind, has raised to 300. This is a very standard opening range from the button. AQo is a premium hand, well ahead of his range. To maximize value and take control of the pot, I will put in a 3-bet. A standard 3-bet size here would be around 3x to 4x his open, so I will raise to 1000.

Facing a 3-bet to 1000 (3.3x over my 300 open) from the BB while holding QJo offsuit OOP as SB. QJo is a decent opening hand but marginal against a 3-bet range, lacking strong equity, blockers, or playability postflop out of position.
